the inline session push; the bot sandbox now allows
  `~/.config/jj`, `/tmp/`, and `~/.cargo` writes, so tests
  and `cargo install` run sandboxed (fixed 2026-07-15)

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](/AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **vc-x1 push: pause point between commit and publish
   stages.** The merge non-ff close-out is a three-step
   sequence:
   - commit the close-out pair locally (normal 1:1 commit
     stages, ochids injected both directions)
   - rebase the code-side close-out into the merge (chids
     survive rebase, so every ochid stays valid)
   - publish

   Push has no supported stop after `commit-claude`, so today
   the recipe pre-commits both sides manually and resumes via
   `--from bookmark-set --yes` — skipping exactly the stages
   that inject `ochid:` trailers.
   - Add a stop after the commit stages (`--to commit-claude`
     or `--no-publish`; name open); the existing `--from
     bookmark-set` is already the resume half.
   - Retires the close-out workaround; the merge commit
     carries its code→bot ochid because it was injected
     normally, before the rebase.
   - Together with the Todo "push/sync: bookmark is
     code-repo-only; pin the bot repo to main", completes
     the trapezoidal-commit workflow (1:1 bot↔code
     throughout; the merge is a code-side-only shape
     operation).

2. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
   But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
   merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change
   ships under is only fixed when it merges into `main`,
   so the landing version can't be anticipated while the
   work is underway. Pervasive version-in-text is
   therefore fragile for any non-linear / multi-contributor
   workflow. Promoted from Ideas at 0.65.2-0; slated for
   the cycle after 0.65.2.
   - Open question: what identifies a cycle's commits if
     not a pre-assigned version?
     - Needs to be unique within some agreed upon domain.
       A contributors email address would do it, but also
       a UUID (short-version) for a contribution. I could
       imagine a UUID generated from the initial email/issue
       that and then "version number" schema appended to that.
   - Surfaces to update once the identifier is chosen:
     cycle-protocol.md (title shape, Numbering), AGENTS.md
     (commit-recording headers), and the `vc-x1` validators
     that parse `(X.Y.Z)` strings.
3. **sync follow-up: extract `move-bookmark` command.** The
   "put the bookmark / `@` where it belongs" step at the end
   of sync (reposition logic) is useful standalone — e.g. the
   t1B scenario where `main` is right but `@` isn't on it —
   and deserves an honestly-named command instead of a mode.
   - `vc-x1 move-bookmark` (name open): no fetch; move `@`
     (and optionally the bookmark) onto a target under the
     same safety rules as sync's reposition step.
   - Sync's final step becomes a call to the same logic.
   - Follow-up to the 0.67.0 single-mode sync cycle.
4. **sync follow-up: push preflight in-process; drop
   `--check`; revisit push auto-rollback.** Push's preflight
   shells out to `vc-x1 sync --check` — a verify-only pass
   that is both racy (remote can move before the user's
   later apply) and not actually read-only (jj's fetch
   auto-ffs tracked bookmarks). Follow-up to the 0.67.0
   single-mode sync cycle.
   - Preflight becomes a real in-process sync (stop-on-error
     halts the push before anything is committed); the
     `sync --check` shell-out and PATH dependency go away.
   - Remove the deprecated hidden `--check` alias once
     nothing invokes it.
   - Apply the stop-on-error + `vc-x1 revert` philosophy to
     push's commit-stage rollback (today it auto-runs
     `jj op restore`, hiding the evidence).
5. **validate-numbering: rename the pair, check all
   sequence-managed notes files generically.** `validate-todo`
   / `fix-todo` only operate on the single file passed, so a
   renumber slip in `bugs.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, or
   `todo.md`'s `## Ideas` section passes unnoticed — too weak
   for a pre-commit gate. Prereq for the pre-commit doc
   validators (Todo "pre-commit: single rule ...").
   - Rename the pair: `validate-todo` → `validate-numbering`,
     `fix-todo` → `fix-numbering` — they validate numbered-
     sequence integrity, not todos specifically.
   - Generic detection: for every `#…#` section, validate the
     column-0 `^\d+\.␠` entries form a contiguous 1..N run.
     Drops the Todo/Bugs special-casing; auto-covers
     `## Ideas` and any new numbered section. Keep the
     column-0 anchor so indented sub-lists aren't counted.
   - Default scope: a fixed list of sequence-managed notes
     files (`todo.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, `bugs.md`) so the
     no-arg pre-commit run covers them all. Fixed rather than
     a `notes/**.md` walk because prose docs
     (`cycle-protocol.md`, design notes) carry ordinary
     numbered lists that aren't managed sequences — a walk
     would false-positive (markdown renders `1. 1. 1.` as
     1-2-3, a legitimate prose pattern).
   - Override args follow the `--init-from` convention:
     positional files/dirs (a dir → its `*.md`) plus an
     `@<file>` manifest, additive — for ad-hoc validation of
     a specific file.

input.description: See remaining Todo entries and Ideas boundary

content.content:
- Open: revisit fixed-vs-glob at implementation if the
     fixed list proves annoying to maintain.
6. **pre-commit: single rule (no docs skip) + doc validators.**
   The pre-commit (cargo cycle: fmt/clippy/test/install) only
   checks code, so it's "skip-able for purely-docs commits" —
   but that exception is exactly where checks slip (skipped on
   0.62.0-7/-8 until caught). And `vc-x1 push`'s `preflight`
   stage re-runs the same cycle, which invites treating push as
   the gate rather than a redundant safety-net.
   - Adopt one rule, no exception: the pre-commit runs before
     Work review on every commit; push's `preflight` is a
     safety-net, not the primary gate. (docs: AGENTS.md Cycle
     Protocol summary + cycle-protocol.md per-commit-flow.)
   - Enrich the pre-commit so it's meaningful on docs commits:
     add the doc validators — `validate-numbering` (its own
     Todo, a prereq) plus `validate-repo` when it exists — to
     both the documented flow and push's `preflight` stage
     (`push.rs`), with a test. (code)
   - This dissolves the docs exception: with doc validators in
     the pre-commit there's always something to validate, so
     the carve-out stops making sense.
   - Its own near-term cycle (chosen over a 0.61.1 insert to
     avoid rewriting published 0.62.0-x history); no version
     pre-assigned — see the Todo "Version-number protocol is
     fragile" on fragile version targets.
7. **vc-x1 push: validate body opens with an intro paragraph.**
   A body whose first line is a bullet (`- file: …`) is a
   Prose-Form violation — bodies must open with an intro
   paragraph, then bullets. Today such a body trips jj's arg
   parser (`jj commit -m "<body>"` reads the leading `-` as a
   stray flag) and push fails with an opaque error. Hit on
   0.62.0-5.
   - Feature, not a parser bug (reframed): push should
     *validate* the body opens with a non-dash intro line and
     flag its absence with a clear, specific error pointing at
     the offending first line — rather than letting jj emit a
     confusing one, or quietly accepting a bullet-first body.
   - Enforcing the intro is the intended behavior, matching
     the Prose-Form convention; we are not "fixing" the parser
     to accept bullet-first bodies.
   - Workaround until the explicit check lands: prepend a
     non-dash intro sentence to the body.
8. **vc-x1 push: record uncovered code commits (N:1 code↔bot).**
   Today push assumes 1:1 symmetric WC commits with shared
   title/body. The interop / adoption scenario breaks that:
   the code side is worked single-repo style (commit +
   `jj git push` / `git push`, no `vc-x1 push` in the loop),
   so no bot pairings exist — one bot commit then records
   every code commit not yet covered by a prior `ochid:`,
   via a multi-line `ochid:` per the design in [[10]].
   - Also covers a cycle held local and published all at
     once (the ochid-trailers section's "one ochid per Work
     commit" on merge close-out) — work commits never
     individually paired.
   - Out of scope: the trapezoid close-out. That flow stays
     1:1 (the close-out pair commits normally, then the
     merge rebase; chids survive rebase, so ochids stay
     valid); its enabler is the Todo "vc-x1 push: pause
     point between commit and publish stages".
   - Teach push to:
     - detect the shape (code WC empty, uncovered commits at
       the bookmark)
     - skip `commit-app`
     - compose a `.claude`-specific message
     - emit one `ochid:` line per uncovered commit
   - Open: computing "uncovered" — likely a revset from the
     code bookmark back to the newest commit referenced by
     the bot journal's ochids.
9. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
   `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
   (`init.rs`, tests).

   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
   of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
   issue.
10. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`

toolUseResult.stdout:
- Open: revisit fixed-vs-glob at implementation if the
     fixed list proves annoying to maintain.
6. **pre-commit: single rule (no docs skip) + doc validators.**
   The pre-commit (cargo cycle: fmt/clippy/test/install) only
   checks code, so it's "skip-able for purely-docs commits" —
   but that exception is exactly where checks slip (skipped on
   0.62.0-7/-8 until caught). And `vc-x1 push`'s `preflight`
   stage re-runs the same cycle, which invites treating push as
   the gate rather than a redundant safety-net.
   - Adopt one rule, no exception: the pre-commit runs before
     Work review on every commit; push's `preflight` is a
     safety-net, not the primary gate. (docs: AGENTS.md Cycle
     Protocol summary + cycle-protocol.md per-commit-flow.)
   - Enrich the pre-commit so it's meaningful on docs commits:
     add the doc validators — `validate-numbering` (its own
     Todo, a prereq) plus `validate-repo` when it exists — to
     both the documented flow and push's `preflight` stage
     (`push.rs`), with a test. (code)
   - This dissolves the docs exception: with doc validators in
     the pre-commit there's always something to validate, so
     the carve-out stops making sense.
   - Its own near-term cycle (chosen over a 0.61.1 insert to
     avoid rewriting published 0.62.0-x history); no version
     pre-assigned — see the Todo "Version-number protocol is
     fragile" on fragile version targets.
7. **vc-x1 push: validate body opens with an intro paragraph.**
   A body whose first line is a bullet (`- file: …`) is a
   Prose-Form violation — bodies must open with an intro
   paragraph, then bullets. Today such a body trips jj's arg
   parser (`jj commit -m "<body>"` reads the leading `-` as a
   stray flag) and push fails with an opaque error. Hit on
   0.62.0-5.
   - Feature, not a parser bug (reframed): push should
     *validate* the body opens with a non-dash intro line and
     flag its absence with a clear, specific error pointing at
     the offending first line — rather than letting jj emit a
     confusing one, or quietly accepting a bullet-first body.
   - Enforcing the intro is the intended behavior, matching
     the Prose-Form convention; we are not "fixing" the parser
     to accept bullet-first bodies.
   - Workaround until the explicit check lands: prepend a
     non-dash intro sentence to the body.
8. **vc-x1 push: record uncovered code commits (N:1 code↔bot).**
   Today push assumes 1:1 symmetric WC commits with shared
   title/body. The interop / adoption scenario breaks that:
   the code side is worked single-repo style (commit +
   `jj git push` / `git push`, no `vc-x1 push` in the loop),
   so no bot pairings exist — one bot commit then records
   every code commit not yet covered by a prior `ochid:`,
   via a multi-line `ochid:` per the design in [[10]].
   - Also covers a cycle held local and published all at
     once (the ochid-trailers section's "one ochid per Work
     commit" on merge close-out) — work commits never
     individually paired.
   - Out of scope: the trapezoid close-out. That flow stays
     1:1 (the close-out pair commits normally, then the
     merge rebase; chids survive rebase, so ochids stay
     valid); its enabler is the Todo "vc-x1 push: pause
     point between commit and publish stages".
   - Teach push to:
     - detect the shape (code WC empty, uncovered commits at
       the bookmark)
     - skip `commit-app`
     - compose a `.claude`-specific message
     - emit one `ochid:` line per uncovered commit
   - Open: computing "uncovered" — likely a revset from the
     code bookmark back to the newest commit referenced by
     the bot journal's ochids.
9. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
   `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
   its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
   so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
   on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
   leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,

   Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead

   ~30 call sites hand-roll `run("jj", ["log", "-r", <rev>,

     `desc_of`, `is_empty`

     parsers will eventually disagree

     `squash_push::extract_ochids` vs `common::extract_ochid`
     (jj-lib `Commit`-based); one string-level parser in
     `desc_helpers` both can call
   - test dedup: promote the near-identical `jj()` / `cid()`
     / `chid()` / `description()` helpers from

10. **Split push.rs state machinery; document the jj-lib vs
   subprocess rule.** `push.rs` (~1.6k lines, the largest
   file) holds the `Stage` machine, TOML state persistence,
   eight stage bodies, two sanity verifiers, and the
   interactive gates; `sync/state.rs` is the in-repo
   precedent for splitting.
   - extract `push/state.rs`: `Stage`, `PushState`, state
     layout resolution
   - ARCHITECTURE.md: state the implicit access rule —
     read-only display commands (`chid`, `desc`, `list`,
     `show`, `validate-desc`) use jj-lib in-process;
     mutating commands (`init`, `sync`, `push`,
     `squash-push`) shell out to `jj` — so new subcommands
     don't re-decide it ad hoc
11. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**

content.text:
Both entries are in and validated — 13 Todo entries, all sequential:

- **#9 "Dedup jj subprocess queries behind a typed facade"** — the ~30 hand-rolled `jj log -T` call sites and per-module private wrappers, with sub-bullets for the facade shape, folding `bm_track_one` onto `verify_tracking`'s parser, unifying the two ochid trailer parsers, and promoting the triplicated test helpers (`jj`/`cid`/`chid`/`description`) into `test_helpers.rs`. It records your observation directly: at 0.69.0-2, `jj_rev_exists` read as "first of its kind" but is the Nth reinvention — that's exactly the failure mode the facade prevents, since the next author would find `jj::rev_exists` instead of writing another.
- **#10 "Split push.rs state machinery; document the jj-lib vs subprocess rule"** — the `push/state.rs` extraction (mirroring `sync/state.rs`) and stating the read-only-uses-jj-lib / mutating-shells-out rule in ARCHITECTURE.md so new subcommands don't re-decide it ad hoc.

`fix-todo` renumbered the downstream entries (`por → dual` is now #12, desc equalization #13) and also normalized the two-digit entries' continuation indent.

That's everything from this review round. The -2 diff now spans the rename, the marker-machinery removal, the help-text precision pass, the cycle-protocol/AGENTS.md doc updates, the bugs.md prune, and these two Todo entries. Ready to draft the commit description when you are.

content.text:
Here's the draft:

**Title** (46 chars):

```
feat: rename finalize to squash-push (0.69.0-2)
```

Wait — that's 47 with the space before the paren; let me count precisely: `feat: rename finalize to squash-push (0.69.0-2)` = 48 chars. Within the 50 limit.

**Body:**

```
Rename `finalize` to `squash-push`: mechanism-named, repo-generic,
zero-ceremony (bare invocation squashes @ into @- and pushes main
in `.`). The detach machinery retires with it — the command runs
fully in-process, so failures are visible non-zero exits and the
failure-marker files have nothing left to report. Protocol docs
are rewritten for the inline world.

- src/finalize.rs -> src/squash_push.rs
  - SquashPushArgs/SquashPushParams: positional BOOKMARK
    (default main), -R/--repo (default .), --squash pair
    override (default @,@-)
  - retire --detach/--delay/--exec/--push and detach(),
    build_exec_args(), write_failure_marker(),
    surface_previous_failures(), status_dir()
  - finalize_inline dissolves: squash_push() (preflight +
    execute) is the single entry point
  - empty-@ feedback: bookmark at target and remote ->
    "already sync'd", exit 0; remote behind -> skip the
    squash, still push
  - tests rewritten for the new surface; a test pins that
    `finalize` no longer parses (no deprecated alias)
- src/push.rs: Stage::FinalizeClaude -> SquashPushClaude
  ("squash-push-claude"), --no-finalize -> --no-squash-push,
  stage body calls squash_push::squash_push; doc updates
- src/push/tests.rs, src/push/integration_tests.rs: rename
  sweep; stage tests renamed push_squash_push_claude_*
- src/main.rs: Commands::SquashPush wiring + long_about;
  push help title made precise ("Commit both repos, push the
  work repo, squash-push the bot repo"); squash-push short
  help generic (SOURCE/TARGET); surface_previous_failures
  call removed
- src/context.rs: drop the log field and load(log) param —
  its only reader was the detach re-exec
- src/subcommand.rs, src/common.rs, src/test_helpers.rs:
  doc-comment updates for the rename and Context::load()
- tests/cli_sync.rs: --no-finalize -> --no-squash-push
- notes/cycle-protocol.md: stop-and-wait rewritten succinct
  and directive-scoped (intermediate pushes of a delegated
  task proceed; the hard stop lands on the turn's final
  push); Recovery recipe is now `vc-x1 squash-push -R
  .claude` + stale pre-0.69.0-2 state-file caveat
- notes/bugs.md: prune #3 (surface_previous_failures racy)
  as moot; renumber
- notes/todo.md: -2 marked done with decisions recorded
  (no alias, markers retired); -4 rescoped to README +
  repo-terminology sweep; new Todos #9 (jj-query facade
  dedup) and #10 (push/state.rs split + jj-lib vs
  subprocess rule)
- AGENTS.md: "finalize" as a plain word reworded to "seal"
  (collides with the retired command name)
- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: version 0.69.0-2
```

Two notes on the draft: I used ASCII `->` in the body to avoid arrow-glyph clutter in `git log` output, matching the title's plain style — say the word if you prefer `→` like some earlier bodies used. And per protocol the `ochid:` trailer is omitted; `vc-x1 push` injects it.

message.content: Thoughts on renaming SquashPushClaude to SquashPushBotRepo or ..

content.text:
Worth doing, but not alone and not in this commit. My thoughts:

**The inconsistency argument cuts both ways.** `SquashPushClaude` sits in a family: `CommitApp`, `CommitClaude`, `BookmarkSet`, `PushApp`. Renaming just it to `SquashPushBotRepo` would make the stage vocabulary *less* coherent — you'd have "claude", "app", and "bot repo" naming the same two repos across five stages. If the vendor-specific "claude" and the now-deprecated "app" are going away (and per the work-repo/bot-repo decision they should), the right move is one sweep:

- `commit-app` → `commit-work`, `commit-claude` → `commit-bot`
- `push-app` → `push-work`
- `squash-push-claude` → `squash-push-bot`

**Drop the `Repo` suffix.** Every stage operates on a repo; `SquashPushBotRepo`/`squash-push-bot-repo` spends nine characters saying nothing its siblings don't already imply. `SquashPushBot` / `squash-push-bot` reads clean and pairs with `CommitBot`.

**Why not now:** stage names are user-facing surface — `--from` values, `--status` output, the persisted state file, help text, `verify_state_sanity` messages, and a matching sweep through `stage_*` function names and tests. Bundling that into -2 would bury the finalize→squash-push rename under a second rename with its own compat caveat (another stale-state-file generation). It's exactly the shape of the -4 terminology sweep, which already covers "code doc comments" — stage identifiers are the natural extension.

One genuine counterpoint to weigh: the repo directory is literally `.claude`, so `commit-claude` is arguably *accurate* in a way "bot" abstracts over. But the project already committed to "bot repo" as the concept name, and the path is an implementation detail the config (`.vc-config.toml`) could someday move.

Want me to extend the -4 Todo bullet to include the stage-name sweep (`app`/`claude` → `work`/`bot` across the Stage enum, stage strings, and their call sites), with `squash-push-bot` as the chosen name?

content.text: Agreed — rename it now while it's a day old. Going with `SquashPushBot` / `squash-push-bot` (the `Repo` suffix adds nothing its siblings don't imply). The remaining `app`/`claude` stages join the -4 sweep, and I'll capture the ".claude doesn't need to be .claude" intent as a Todo. Finding all the sites:

41	/// Banner string emitted as the first line of normal command runs
42	/// and shown at the top of subcommand `--help` output. Built from
43	/// `Cargo.toml`'s name + version at compile time so it stays in
44	/// sync with the bumped version.
45	const BANNER: &str = concat!(env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"), " ", env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"));
46	
47	/// Top-level about line — name, version, and the project tagline
48	/// on a single line. Used as the top-level `about` so `vc-x1 -h`
49	/// reads as one banner-plus-tagline header instead of two stacked
50	/// lines.
51	const TOP_ABOUT: &str = concat!(
52	    env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"),
53	    " ",
54	    env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"),
55	    " - jj workspace tooling"
56	);
57	
58	/// Build the clap command tree with `BANNER` set as `before_help`
59	/// on every subcommand (transitively). Top-level skips `before_help`
60	/// because its own `about` already carries the name+version+tagline.
61	/// Walks via `mut_subcommand` so individual subcommand
62	/// `#[command(long_about = ...)]` blocks don't have to repeat the
63	/// banner text.
64	fn cli_with_banner() -> clap::Command {
65	    fn add_to_subs(mut cmd: clap::Command) -> clap::Command {
66	        let names: Vec<String> = cmd
67	            .get_subcommands()
68	            .map(|c| c.get_name().to_string())
69	            .collect();
70	        for name in names {
71	            cmd = cmd.mut_subcommand(name, add_with_banner);
72	        }
73	        cmd
74	    }
75	    fn add_with_banner(mut cmd: clap::Command) -> clap::Command {
76	        cmd = cmd.before_help(BANNER);
77	        let names: Vec<String> = cmd
78	            .get_subcommands()
79	            .map(|c| c.get_name().to_string())
80	            .collect();
81	        for name in names {
82	            cmd = cmd.mut_subcommand(name, add_with_banner);
83	        }
84	        cmd
85	    }
86	    add_to_subs(Cli::command())
87	}

1	//! `push` subcommand — collapse the dual-repo commit+push+
2	//! squash-push ceremony into a single resumable command.
3	//!
4	//! See `notes/chores/chores-05.md > Add push subcommand (0.37.0)` for the
5	//! full design.
6	//!
7	//! Dev-step ladder (expanded from original 4 to 6 after adding an
8	//! integration-test step ahead of the first dogfood):
9	//!
10	//! - `0.37.0-0` — scaffolding: flag surface, `Stage` enum, stub `push()`
11	//! - `0.37.0-1` — state file + stage-dispatch loop with stage stubs;
12	//!   `--status`, `--restart`, `--from`
13	//! - `0.37.0-2` — real stage bodies (commits, bookmarks, push,
14	//!   finalize) + `jj op` snapshot rollback
15	//! - `0.37.0-3` — integration tests + workspace-root refactor
16	//!   (thread `root: &Path` through every stage so fixtures can
17	//!   point them at tempdirs); first `vc-x1 push` dogfood ships
18	//!   this commit
19	//! - `0.37.0-4` — interactivity: two approval gates, `$EDITOR`,
20	//!   message persistence across resumes
21	//! - `0.37.0-5` — polish: `--dry-run`, `--step`, non-tty handling,
22	//!   `.gitignore` coherence warning
23	//! - `0.37.0` — docs + workflow migration (done marker)

39	
40	/// Bookmark the session (`.claude`) repo always advances and
41	/// pushes. The session repo is a linear journal on `main` by
42	/// design — `<bookmark>` names a code-repo bookmark only.
43	const SESSION_BOOKMARK: &str = "main";
44	
45	/// Named stages of the `push` state machine.
46	///
47	/// Used by `--from <stage>` to resume at a specific point and by
48	/// `--status` to report the current position. Ordered top-down so
49	/// `Stage as u8` comparisons reflect progress through the flow.
50	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, ValueEnum)]
51	#[value(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
52	pub enum Stage {
53	    /// Run fmt / clippy / test / install / retest.
54	    Preflight,
55	    /// Present diff for the first approval gate.
56	    Review,
57	    /// Compose / edit the commit message; present for second gate.
58	    Message,
59	    /// Commit the app repo.
60	    CommitApp,
61	    /// Commit the `.claude` session repo (skipped if empty).
62	    CommitClaude,
63	    /// Advance each repo's bookmark to its `@-`: app → `<bookmark>`,
64	    /// session → `main`.
65	    BookmarkSet,
66	    /// `jj git push --bookmark <b> -R .`.
67	    PushApp,
68	    /// In-process squash of `.claude`'s trailing session writes
69	    /// + push `main`.
70	    SquashPushBot,
71	}
72	
73	impl Stage {
74	    /// Return the stage's kebab-case string identifier (matches what
75	    /// the CLI accepts via `--from <stage>` and what `PushState`
76	    /// persists to disk).
77	    pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
78	        match self {
79	            Stage::Preflight => "preflight",
80	            Stage::Review => "review",
81	            Stage::Message => "message",
82	            Stage::CommitApp => "commit-app",
83	            Stage::CommitClaude => "commit-claude",
84	            Stage::BookmarkSet => "bookmark-set",
85	            Stage::PushApp => "push-app",
86	            Stage::SquashPushBot => "squash-push-bot",
87	        }
88	    }
89	
90	    /// Parse a kebab-case stage name back into a `Stage`.
91	    ///
92	    /// Unknown names return `None`; callers should surface a helpful
93	    /// error rather than silently substituting a default.
94	    pub fn from_str(name: &str) -> Option<Self> {

content.content:
10. **Split push.rs state machinery; document the jj-lib vs
    subprocess rule.** `push.rs` (~1.6k lines, the largest
    file) holds the `Stage` machine, TOML state persistence,
    eight stage bodies, two sanity verifiers, and the
    interactive gates; `sync/state.rs` is the in-repo
    precedent for splitting.
    - extract `push/state.rs`: `Stage`, `PushState`, state
      layout resolution
    - ARCHITECTURE.md: state the implicit access rule —
      read-only display commands (`chid`, `desc`, `list`,
      `show`, `validate-desc`) use jj-lib in-process;
      mutating commands (`init`, `sync`, `push`,
      `squash-push`) shell out to `jj` — so new subcommands
      don't re-decide it ad hoc
11. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**

11. **Make the bot repo directory name configurable (not
    hardcoded `.claude`).** The bot repo lives at `.claude`
    because that is where Claude Code keeps session data,
    but the concept is agent-agnostic and the user wants to
    change it (decided 2026-07-15: the name does not need to
    be `.claude`). Today the literal is embedded across
    init/clone/push/sync, `claude_path()`, ochid path
    semantics (`/.claude/<chid>`), the
    `~/.claude/projects/<path>` symlink, and docs.
    - `.vc-config.toml` already records each repo's
      workspace path — likely the natural home for the
      configurable name.
    - Open: migration for existing workspaces, the ochid
      trailer prefix (recorded in immutable history), and
      the Claude Code symlink whose location the harness
      controls.
12. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**

24	command exit — silently, every cycle (see Bugs #1 [[24]]). The
25	empty-@ goal behind the detach/delay dance is self-referential
26	for the bot (finalizing is itself session data), so the bot
27	forgoes it; only the user, acting after the turn, can capture

35	  - occasionally useful on the app repo: amend-and-push —

38	  so its push is a forced update
39	  - functions fine whether the bot or the user runs it
40	  - but on the bot repo only the user gets an empty `@`:
41	    the bot's own invocation writes new session data, so
42	    its `@` is non-empty again immediately
43	  - on any repo without a live writer it works as expected
44	    for anyone

53	  - `@` empty but remote behind → skip the squash, still

The stage is now `Stage::SquashPushBot` / `squash-push-bot` everywhere — code, tests, help text, cycle-protocol.md — and the bookkeeping is recorded: the -2 ladder bullet notes it's the first use of the work/bot stage vocabulary, -4 gained the matching sweep for the remaining stages (`commit-app`→`commit-work`, `commit-claude`→`commit-bot`, `push-app`→`push-work`), and your ".claude doesn't need to be .claude" intent is captured as Todo #11 (configurable bot-repo directory name, with the migration/ochid-prefix/symlink open questions noted).

Revised commit description:

**Title** (48 chars):

```
feat: rename finalize to squash-push (0.69.0-2)
```

**Body:**

```
Rename `finalize` to `squash-push`: mechanism-named, repo-generic,
zero-ceremony (bare invocation squashes @ into @- and pushes main
in `.`). The detach machinery retires with it — the command runs
fully in-process, so failures are visible non-zero exits and the
failure-marker files have nothing left to report. Push's session
stage is renamed `squash-push-bot`, the first use of the work/bot
stage vocabulary. Protocol docs are rewritten for the inline world.

- src/finalize.rs -> src/squash_push.rs
  - SquashPushArgs/SquashPushParams: positional BOOKMARK
    (default main), -R/--repo (default .), --squash pair
    override (default @,@-)
  - retire --detach/--delay/--exec/--push and detach(),
    build_exec_args(), write_failure_marker(),
    surface_previous_failures(), status_dir()
  - finalize_inline dissolves: squash_push() (preflight +
    execute) is the single entry point
  - empty-@ feedback: bookmark at target and remote ->
    "already sync'd", exit 0; remote behind -> skip the
    squash, still push
  - tests rewritten for the new surface; a test pins that
    `finalize` no longer parses (no deprecated alias)
- src/push.rs: Stage::FinalizeClaude -> SquashPushBot
  ("squash-push-bot"), --no-finalize -> --no-squash-push,
  stage body calls squash_push::squash_push; doc updates
- src/push/tests.rs, src/push/integration_tests.rs: rename
  sweep; stage tests renamed push_squash_push_bot_*
- src/main.rs: Commands::SquashPush wiring + long_about;
  push help title made precise ("Commit both repos, push the
  work repo, squash-push the bot repo"); squash-push short
  help generic (SOURCE/TARGET); surface_previous_failures
  call removed
- src/context.rs: drop the log field and load(log) param —
  its only reader was the detach re-exec
- src/subcommand.rs, src/common.rs, src/test_helpers.rs:
  doc-comment updates for the rename and Context::load()
- tests/cli_sync.rs: --no-finalize -> --no-squash-push
- notes/cycle-protocol.md: stop-and-wait rewritten succinct
  and directive-scoped (intermediate pushes of a delegated
  task proceed; the hard stop lands on the turn's final
  push); Recovery recipe is now `vc-x1 squash-push -R
  .claude` + stale pre-0.69.0-2 state-file caveat
- notes/bugs.md: prune #3 (surface_previous_failures racy)
  as moot; renumber
- notes/todo.md: -2 marked done with decisions recorded;
  -4 rescoped (README, terminology + stage-name sweep); new
  Todos #9 (jj-query facade dedup), #10 (push/state.rs
  split + jj-lib vs subprocess rule), #11 (configurable
  bot-repo directory name)
- AGENTS.md: "finalize" as a plain word reworded to "seal"
  (collides with the retired command name)
- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: version 0.69.0-2
```

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user resumed the vc-x1 project's 0.69.0 cycle at step 0.69.0-2: rename the `finalize` subcommand to `squash-push` per the plan ladder in `notes/todo.md > ## In Progress` ("Continuity (resume 2026-07-15)" notes said: pick up -2, mark it `(current)`, all decisions in Design notes). Through iterative review the user directed: update AGENTS.md and cycle-protocol.md docs now (pulled forward from -4); soften the "After push or squash-push: stop and wait" rule to allow delegated multi-step tasks to proceed past intermediate pushes; adopt "bot repo" / "work repo" terminology (user chose "work repo" from my suggestions); make the section succinct; make `vc-x1 push -h` title precise about what's committed/pushed/squash-pushed; make `squash-push -h` title generic (SOURCE/TARGET not @/@-); remove the `surface_previous_failures` failure-marker machinery entirely (no longer detaching); prune bugs.md #3 (now moot); review code organization/duplication and record findings as Todos; draft the commit description; and rename `Stage::SquashPushClaude` → `Stage::SquashPushBot` now while it's new ("since SquashPush is new I think renaming it now is better than later"). The user also stated the bot repo directory "does not need to be .claude and I want to change it" (captured as a new Todo). Earlier, the user configured permissions: they want `permissions.defaultMode: "acceptEdits"` in user-level `~/.claude/settings.json`, NOT project settings, because they commit the project `.claude` repo and don't want to force their permission defaults on others.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - jj (Jujutsu) VCS: working-copy `@` model, change IDs (chids), bookmarks, `jj squash --ignore-immutable --use-destination-message`, forced-update pushes after rewriting published commits
   - Dual-repo workspace: work repo (project root) + bot repo (`.claude`), `ochid:` trailers cross-linking commits, `.vc-config.toml`
   - Cycle protocol: Preparation (`X.Y.Z-0`) → Work (`-1`, `-2`, …) → Close-out (bare version); per-commit flow: mark (current) → work → flip (done) → cargo cycle (fmt, clippy -D warnings, test, install) → Work review stop → commit description review → commit; title ≤50 chars `<type>: <desc> (<version>)`; body = intro + file-by-file bullets wrap ≤72; ochid trailer injected by `vc-x1 push`, never hand-written
   - "After push or squash-push: stop and wait" rule — now directive-scoped: intermediate pushes of a delegated task proceed; hard stop on the turn's final push
   - clap derive: positional args with defaults, `#[command(flatten)]`, long_about, kebab-case subcommand naming from variant names
   - SubcommandRunner trait pattern: clap Args → clap-free Params via to_params(), dispatch()
   - Push stage state machine: preflight → review → message → commit-app → commit-claude → bookmark-set → push-app → squash-push-bot; persisted in `.vc-x1/push-state.toml`
   - Claude Code sandbox: filesystem allowWrite prefix rules; user added `~/.config/jj`, `/tmp/`, `~/.cargo` to allowWrite in `~/.claude/settings.json`
   - Crate temporarily named `vc-x1-dev` (installs don't clobber stable `vc-x1` used by another bot instance); rename back at close-out
   - Notes conventions: `[[N]]` citations, fix-todo renumbering, prose form (intro + bullets), "The bot thinks..." speculation marker

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `/home/wink/.claude/settings.json` — Added `"defaultMode": "acceptEdits"` to permissions (user-level scope deliberately). User later added to sandbox allowWrite: `/home/wink/.config/jj`, `/tmp/`, `/home/wink/.cargo`.
   - `Cargo.toml` — version bumped to `0.69.0-2`; crate name stays `vc-x1-dev`.
   - `src/squash_push.rs` (renamed from `src/finalize.rs` via `mv` + full rewrite) — The new command module. Key shapes:
     ```rust
     #[derive(Args, Debug)]
     pub struct SquashPushArgs {
         /// Bookmark to advance and push
         #[arg(value_name = "BOOKMARK", default_value = "main")]
         pub bookmark: String,
         /// Path to jj repo
         #[arg(short = 'R', long, default_value = ".")]
         pub repo: PathBuf,
         #[command(flatten)]
         pub squash: SquashOption,
     }
     #[derive(Debug)]
     pub struct SquashPushParams {
         pub repo: PathBuf,
         pub squash: SquashSpec,
         pub bookmark: String,
     }
     ```
     `TryFrom<&SquashPushArgs>` canonicalizes repo, defaults squash to `@,@-` (`// OK: --squash absent → the command's default @,@- pair`). `pub fn squash_push(params)` = preflight + execute: empty-source check via `rev_is_empty_undescribed` (jj `-T empty` + description); no-op "already sync'd" exit 0 when bookmark==target==remote (via `jj_commit_id`, remote via `{bookmark}@origin` with `.unwrap_or_default()`); else squash (skipped if empty) → `jj bookmark set` → `jj git push`. Kept: preflight (revset resolution, `check_squash_keeps_ochids` (Bugs #2), conflicts, bookmark exists/tracking/forward-only, target described), `extract_ochids`, `ochids_at_risk`, `jj_rev_exists`. Removed entirely (second pass per user): `status_dir()`, `surface_previous_failures()`, `write_failure_marker`, `detach()`, `build_exec_args()`. Tests: parse defaults, positional bookmark, all_opts, `--repo` long, bare `--squash`, bad squash, `retired_flags_rejected` (--detach/--delay/--exec/--push), `finalize_subcommand_retired`, try_from defaults, ochid tests.
   - `src/main.rs` — `mod finalize` → `mod squash_push` (alphabetical position); `Commands::SquashPush(squash_push::SquashPushArgs)` with short help "Squash SOURCE into TARGET, advance a bookmark, and push" and long_about opening "Squash SOURCE into TARGET (defaults: SOURCE=@, TARGET=@-), advance a bookmark, and push." + zero-ceremony paragraph; Push short help "Commit both repos, push the work repo, squash-push the bot repo", long_about opener "Commit both repos, push the work repo's BOOKMARK, and squash-push the bot repo's `main` — one resumable command.", stage list ends "squash-push-bot", bookmark-set parenthetical "(work repo → <bookmark>, bot repo → main)"; removed surface_after gate and `surface_previous_failures()` call; `main` returns the dispatch `match` directly (clippy let_and_return); bm_track doc trimmed; `Context::load()` (no arg).
   - `src/push.rs` — `Stage::SquashPushBot` (was FinalizeClaude then SquashPushClaude), as_str `"squash-push-bot"`, from_str, next(); `pub no_squash_push: bool` with doc "Stop before `squash-push-bot` so it can be run manually."; `stage_squash_push_bot(root, _state, params)` builds `SquashPushParams { repo: claude, squash: SquashSpec{@,@-}, bookmark: SESSION_BOOKMARK }` and calls `crate::squash_push::squash_push(&sp)`; log prefix "push squash-push-bot:"; verify_state_sanity matches `Stage::PushApp | Stage::SquashPushBot`; doc updates. Historical mentions (0.37.0 ladder, 0.37.1 incident "finalize-claude") intentionally kept.
   - `src/push/tests.rs`, `src/push/integration_tests.rs` — sed sweeps: no_finalize→no_squash_push, --no-finalize→--no-squash-push, stage strings/variants; test fns `push_squash_push_bot_inline_pushes_session`, `push_squash_push_bot_folds_micro_tail`; fixtures "push-sp-inline"/"push-sp-tail".
   - `src/context.rs` — removed `log: Option<PathBuf>` field and `load(log)` param (only reader was detach re-exec); doc note explains the removal; unused `use std::path::PathBuf` removed.
   - `src/subcommand.rs`, `src/common.rs` (verify_tracking doc: "sync, push, squash-push"), `src/test_helpers.rs` (`Context::load()`, doc "squash-push / push tests") — doc updates.
   - `tests/cli_sync.rs` — `--no-finalize` → `--no-squash-push` in push invocation + docs; "squash-push-bot" mention.
   - `notes/cycle-protocol.md` — "### After push or squash-push: stop and wait" condensed to intro + 3 bullets (**Scope**: stop follows the user's directive; intermediate pushes of delegated work proceed; hard stop on turn's final push. **Why**: bot repo is a live journal; tail rides into next cycle's bot commit between delegated pushes; final push's tail only the user can capture via `vc-x1 squash-push -R .claude`. **Silence**: closing words before final push; known slip). No stage name in that paragraph (user: reads like a command). Recovery: "If push exits before its last stage" with "(`squash-push-bot` in `vc-x1 push --status` / `--from` stage names)", hand recipe `vc-x1 squash-push -R .claude`, dropped Verified-0.64.0 bullet, added pre-0.69.0-2 stale state-file caveat (names retired `finalize-claude` stage → `--restart`); "Late work-repo tweak"; cadence "the squash-push fold".
   - `AGENTS.md` — plain-word "finalize" → "seal" in jj basics (2 spots).
   - `notes/bugs.md` — pruned #3 (`finalize::surface_previous_failures` racy); fix-todo renumbered (init-HEAD now #3, clone-derivation #4); Bugs #1/#2 unchanged so code citations stay valid.
   - `notes/todo.md` — -2 marked `(done)` with decision bullets recorded (failure-markers retired entirely; no `finalize` alias; Context.log removed; stage renamed `squash-push-bot` "first use of the work/bot stage vocabulary; the remaining app/claude stages sweep at -4"; docs pulled forward); -4 rescoped: README + repo-terminology sweep ("bot repo"/"work repo" replace "session repo"/"code repo"/"app repo") + push stage-name sweep (commit-app→commit-work, commit-claude→commit-bot, push-app→push-work); Continuity note updated (sandbox now allows ~/.config/jj, /tmp/, ~/.cargo). New Todos: #9 "Dedup jj subprocess queries behind a typed facade" (~30 call sites, facade `jj_log` + typed helpers, fold bm_track_one onto verify_tracking, unify ochid parsers, promote test jj/cid/chid/description helpers to test_helpers.rs), #10 "Split push.rs state machinery; document the jj-lib vs subprocess rule", #11 "Make the bot repo directory name configurable (not hardcoded `.claude`)". Now 14 Todo entries, validated sequential.

4. Errors and fixes:
   - Permission prompts despite settings change: user restarted session; acceptEdits then worked.
   - clippy `-D warnings`: dead field `Context.log` → removed field + `load()` signature (not `#[allow]`); then unused `PathBuf` import → removed; later `let_and_return` in main after removing surface call → return match directly.
   - Sandboxed test failures: 10 sync integration tests failed (`jj git clone` needs `~/.config/jj` writes, "Read-only file system (os error 30)") → ran unsandboxed; user then interrupted one unsandboxed rerun and instead widened the sandbox (`~/.config/jj`, `/tmp/`); tests then pass sandboxed. `cargo install` similarly needed `~/.cargo` (ran unsandboxed once; user added it).
   - Missed rename site: `tests/cli_sync.rs` (outside src/) invoked `push ... --no-finalize` → 2 cli_sync tests failed with "unexpected argument '--no-finalize'" → renamed there too.
   - Write tool failed on renamed-but-unread file (`mv` then Write) → did a 5-line Read first, then Write succeeded.
   - User corrections/feedback: stop-and-wait rule too strong (added directive-scoped carve-out); "session repo" → "bot repo"/"work repo" (AskUserQuestion → "work repo"); `squash-push-claude` "reads like a command" (dropped from stop-and-wait; framed as stage-name vocabulary in Recovery); "Why" block redundant (condensed section); push -h title nebulous (made precise); squash-push -h title too specific (SOURCE/TARGET generic); surface_previous_failures unnecessary (removed entirely, reversing my keep-one-cycle decision); user thought squash-push-claude didn't exist — I verified it exists in vc-x1-dev while PATH `vc-x1` (0.69.0-0) still shows finalize-claude.

5. Problem Solving:
   Completed the finalize→squash-push rename end-to-end with all design decisions resolved: zero-ceremony CLI, retirement of the detach machinery (including failure markers), no deprecated alias (incompatible flag surface makes clean "unrecognized subcommand" better), stage named `squash-push-bot` adopting the new work/bot vocabulary early. Rewrote protocol docs for the inline world with a directive-scoped stop rule. Performed a codebase organization/duplication review (push.rs 1579 lines largest; ~30 hand-rolled `jj log -T` call sites; triplicated test helpers; two bookmark-tracking parsers; two ochid parsers; implicit jj-lib vs subprocess split) and captured findings as ranked Todos. Cargo cycle green throughout: clippy `-D warnings` clean, 413 tests passing (393 unit + 5+4+5 + 6 cli_sync), `vc-x1-dev` 0.69.0-2 installed.

6. All user messages:
   - "reacquaint and look at the todu and we're resuminmg 0.69.0 and there are some 'Continuity (resume 2026-07-15)' notes in todo"
   - "You changed todo.md and your guards asked for my permission to allow you to change the file. How can I give you carte blanche permissions for you to do anything you want with the contents of this directory?"
   - "If possible I'd prefer to give you permssion in ~/.claude/settings*. The reason is I commit the .claude (the bot repo with your session information) and I'd rather not 'force' other people to default to my permissions."
   - "So you added permissions.defaultMode = acceptEdits to ~/.claude/settings.json what is the 'scope' of that permission?"
   - "In ~/.claude/settings.json we have sandbox.filesystem.allowWrite = './home/wink/data/prgs/rust' does that mean just that directory or the entire tree?"
   - "ok, resume 0.69.0-2"
   - "I'm going to restart you because I still got prompted to give you permissions" / "Continue from where you left off."
   - "I'm back and resumed the session, let's see if you can edit the file"
   - "continue" (after interrupting an unsandboxed test rerun; they had added ~/.config/jj and /tmp/ to sandbox)
   - "We'll need to update AGENTS.md and cycle-protocol.md updated"
   - "The 'After push or squash-push: stop and wait' is to strong, if I want you to complete a mult-step task on your own then you may proceed, how do codify that? In the next paragraph you use 'session repo' let's use 'bot repo' and 'work repo' (typically I've used 'code repo') but we need a more generic term, suggest other choices? You refer to 'its tail stage `squash-push-claude` ..' there is no `squash-push-claude`."
   - (AskUserQuestion answer: "work repo (Recommended)")
   - "But squash-push-claude is not a user facing command, but it read that way to me."
   - "The 'Why (the bot repo is a live journal ...):' seems redundant to the precedeing infomration. I like to make this entire 'After push or squash-push ..' section more succinct, if possible."
   - "The vc-x1 push -h title line is nebulous, be more precise on what's being commited, pushed and squash pushed, currently it says: Dual-repo commit+push+squash-push in one resumable command"
   - "In squash-push -h 'title' it's a little to specific it say '@' into `@-`: ... Maybe something like: Squash <SOURCE> into <TARGET>, advance a bookmark and push. defaults SOURCE=@ TARGET=@- Although maybe a little long, thoughts"
   - "Since this is no longer detaching I don't think we need to 'surface_previous_failures' in any of the code?"
   - "Yes, prune it now" (bugs.md #3)
   - "Take a look at all the code and give me your opinion on its organization and the amount of code that is potnetially duplicated."
   - "Yes, I was looking at squash_push.rs and saw `fn jj_rev_exists` and thought this is the first time a function like that is needed, hence the question?"
   - "draft the commit description"
   - "Thoughts on renaming SquashPushClaude to SquashPushBotRepo or .."
   - "It does not need to be .claude and I want to change it, and since SquashPush is new I think renaming it now is better than latter, assuming we pick a 'good' name."

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Present the revised commit description for 0.69.0-2 (previous draft is stale: it says `squash-push-claude`/`SquashPushClaude`; must now say `squash-push-bot`/`SquashPushBot` and mention Todo #11 and the -4 stage-sweep rescope). Then Commit Description review → commit (`jj commit -m title -m body -R .` or via `vc-x1 push`; do NOT hand-write ochid trailers — push injects them).
   - Remaining cycle ladder: 0.69.0-3 (push preflight backstop: error/auto-push when `.claude main` ahead of `main@origin`; tests), 0.69.0-4 (docs: README; repo-terminology sweep "bot repo"/"work repo"; push stage-name sweep commit-app→commit-work, commit-claude→commit-bot, push-app→push-work), 0.69.0 close-out (also decide crate rename back to `vc-x1`).
   - Cycle pushes go straight to `main` (keep-separate shape).
   - Protocol constraints to preserve: stop after push (directive-scoped — the hard stop lands on the turn's final push); all closing words before the final push invoke; work review stop before writing descriptions; ask on deviations.

8. Current Work:
   The user approved renaming the push stage now: "since SquashPush is new I think renaming it now is better than latter". I executed the sed sweep `squash-push-claude`→`squash-push-bot`, `SquashPushClaude`→`SquashPushBot`, `stage_squash_push_claude`→`stage_squash_push_bot` across `src/push.rs`, `src/push/tests.rs`, `src/push/integration_tests.rs`, `src/main.rs`, `src/squash_push.rs`, `tests/cli_sync.rs`, `notes/cycle-protocol.md`; updated the todo.md -2 ladder bullet ("first use of the work/bot stage vocabulary; the remaining app/claude stages sweep at -4"), extended the -4 docs bullet with the stage-name sweep, added Todo #11 "Make the bot repo directory name configurable (not hardcoded `.claude`)" (capturing the user's stated intent to change `.claude`), ran fix-todo (14 entries, sequential). The final cargo cycle just completed green: clippy clean, all test suites pass (393/5/4/5/6), `vc-x1-dev` 0.69.0-2 reinstalled. I had not yet reported these results to the user or re-presented the commit description.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Report the stage-rename completion (all green) and present the **revised** commit description draft — the user's last standing request was "draft the commit description", and the draft I presented earlier is now stale after the `squash-push-bot` rename. Update it to: title `feat: rename finalize to squash-push (0.69.0-2)` (unchanged), and body edits — push.rs bullet becomes "Stage::FinalizeClaude -> SquashPushBot (\"squash-push-bot\") — first use of the work/bot stage vocabulary", test bullet "push_squash_push_bot_*", main.rs stage-list mention "squash-push-bot", cycle-protocol.md Recovery mention updated, and todo.md bullet extended to note Todo #11 (configurable bot-repo dir) and the -4 stage-sweep rescope. Then stop for the user's Commit Description review per the per-commit flow.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /home/wink/.claude/projects/-home-wink-data-prgs-rust-vc-x1/3e9f6a90-bbfc-4efb-8aaa-7a5f81d9a42d.jsonl
Continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with "I'll continue" or similar. Pick up the last task as if the break never happened.

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - Messages API for directly passing messages to Claude, Tool Runner (`client.beta.messages.tool_runner`) for running an agentic loop over your own tools, manual tool-use loops, Managed Agents for server-hosted agents with a managed sandbox, prompt caching, and general Anthropic SDK usage; (4) Claude Tag (Claude in Slack) - what it is, setting it up for a Slack workspace, `/install-slack-app`. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

## claude-in-chrome
**IMPORTANT: If the Chrome browser tools are deferred (must be loaded via ToolSearch before use), load them with ToolSearch before calling them, and batch every tool you expect to need into ONE ToolSearch call (the select query accepts a comma-separated list). Do NOT load tools one at a time; each separate ToolSearch call wastes a full round-trip.**

Start a browser task whose tools are not yet loaded with a single call loading the core set:

ToolSearch with query "select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp,mcp__claude-in-chrome__navigate,mcp__claude-in-chrome__computer,mcp__claude-in-chrome__read_page,mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_create_mcp"

Add task-specific tools to the same call when the task obviously needs them: read_console_messages / read_network_requests for debugging, form_input for forms, gif_creator for recordings, javascript_tool for page scripting. Only issue a second ToolSearch if the task later needs a tool you did not anticipate.

280	At close-out the cycle's *work* is done; its *published
281	shape* is the remaining choice, made at push time. Surface
282	the options and get user approval before pushing — once on
283	the target, changing shape is a remote rewrite (force-push,
284	needs approval), so choose deliberately.
285	
286	- **Squash to one commit** — single entry on the target.
287	  Right for straightforward changes where the Work-N is
288	  focused on one or two files.
289	- **Merge non-ff** *(current default)* — `main` gains a
290	  merge commit (`X.Y.Z`); cycle commits stay reachable via
291	  two parents. `jj log -r ..@ -n <N>` shows the trapezoidal
292	  shape. See [Merge non-ff recipe](#merge-non-ff-recipe)
293	  for the full setup sequence.
294	- **Keep separate** — one commit per cycle entry on
295	  `main`. Use when the decomposition itself is
296	  informative. Each chores section keeps its own header /
297	  `Commits:` ref; no consolidation churn.
298	
299	Set up squash/merge before invoking `vc-x1 push`; use
300	`jj git push` directly for non-standard shapes.
301	
302	### Merge non-ff recipe
303	
304	Setting up a [Merge non-ff](#shape-at-close-out-push)
305	close-out is a fixed sequence. `<closeout>` is the cycle's
306	close-out commit, `<prev>` the previous cycle's close-out
307	(the current `main` tip), `<work-tip>` the cycle's last
308	Work commit:
309	
310	1. **Rebase the close-out into a merge** — `jj rebase -r
311	   <closeout> --onto <prev> --onto <work-tip>`.
312	   - `-r <closeout>` keeps the `<closeout>` commit in place.
313	   - `--onto <prev>` becomes its first parent (trunk).
314	   - `--onto <work-tip>` becomes the second parent.
315	
316	   Together these make `<closeout>` a merge of
317	   `<prev>` + `<work-tip>`, forming a trapezoidal commit.
318	2. **Use `jj new <merge>`** to add an empty `@` above the
319	   merge. The rebase left `@` *on* the now-content-bearing
320	   merge, which git/IDE diff views show as uncommitted;
321	   `jj new` restores the clean empty `@` on top.
322	3. **Push** — `jj git push --bookmark main -R .`.
323	
324	**Post-hoc caveat.** If the cycle was already pushed
325	[Keep separate](#shape-at-close-out-push) its commits are
326	immutable: the rebase needs `--ignore-immutable` and the
327	push force-updates `main`.
328	The standard sequence assumes the merge is set up *before*
329	the close-out push.
330	
331	### vc-x1 push wrapper
332	
333	`vc-x1 push <bookmark>` wraps per-push mechanics. See
334	`vc-x1 push --help` for current flags.
335	
336	`vc-x1 push` injects the `ochid:` trailers itself (the
337	`commit-app` / `commit-claude` stages append them) — don't
338	hand-write them into the commit body or `--title`/`--body`.
339	
340	**Current limitation**: only fully supports the
341	[Keep separate](#shape-at-close-out-push) shape; other
342	shapes need manual jj steps. Improvements tracked /
343	planned:
344	
345	- Merge non-ff close-out without the manual pre-commits
346	  (`## Todo` entry "vc-x1 push: pause point between commit
347	  and publish stages") — commit stages run normally, pause
348	  for the merge rebase, resume via `--from bookmark-set`.
349	- N:1 code↔bot for code worked outside vc-x1 (`## Todo`
350	  entry "vc-x1 push: record uncovered code commits").
351	- Symmetric squash — demoted to `todo-backlog.md`

356	Landed: per-repo bookmark names (0.68.0) — `<bookmark>` is
357	code-repo-only; the session repo is pinned to `main`

359	
360	### .claude cadence
361	
362	**Cadence**: one push = one `.claude` commit, paired
363	with every code commit in that push.
364	
365	The `.claude` working copy accumulates session data
366	across the cycle; its change ID stays stable across
367	snapshots, `jj describe`, and the squash-push fold, so

file.content:
At close-out the cycle's *work* is done; its *published
shape* is the remaining choice, made at push time. Surface
the options and get user approval before pushing — once on
the target, changing shape is a remote rewrite (force-push,
needs approval), so choose deliberately.

- **Squash to one commit** — single entry on the target.
  Right for straightforward changes where the Work-N is
  focused on one or two files.
- **Merge non-ff** *(current default)* — `main` gains a
  merge commit (`X.Y.Z`); cycle commits stay reachable via
  two parents. `jj log -r ..@ -n <N>` shows the trapezoidal
  shape. See [Merge non-ff recipe](#merge-non-ff-recipe)
  for the full setup sequence.
- **Keep separate** — one commit per cycle entry on
  `main`. Use when the decomposition itself is
  informative. Each chores section keeps its own header /
  `Commits:` ref; no consolidation churn.

Set up squash/merge before invoking `vc-x1 push`; use
`jj git push` directly for non-standard shapes.

### Merge non-ff recipe

Setting up a [Merge non-ff](#shape-at-close-out-push)
close-out is a fixed sequence. `<closeout>` is the cycle's
close-out commit, `<prev>` the previous cycle's close-out
(the current `main` tip), `<work-tip>` the cycle's last
Work commit:

1. **Rebase the close-out into a merge** — `jj rebase -r
   <closeout> --onto <prev> --onto <work-tip>`.
   - `-r <closeout>` keeps the `<closeout>` commit in place.
   - `--onto <prev>` becomes its first parent (trunk).
   - `--onto <work-tip>` becomes the second parent.

   Together these make `<closeout>` a merge of
   `<prev>` + `<work-tip>`, forming a trapezoidal commit.
2. **Use `jj new <merge>`** to add an empty `@` above the
   merge. The rebase left `@` *on* the now-content-bearing
   merge, which git/IDE diff views show as uncommitted;
   `jj new` restores the clean empty `@` on top.
3. **Push** — `jj git push --bookmark main -R .`.

**Post-hoc caveat.** If the cycle was already pushed
[Keep separate](#shape-at-close-out-push) its commits are
immutable: the rebase needs `--ignore-immutable` and the
push force-updates `main`.
The standard sequence assumes the merge is set up *before*
the close-out push.

### vc-x1 push wrapper

`vc-x1 push <bookmark>` wraps per-push mechanics. See
`vc-x1 push --help` for current flags.

`vc-x1 push` injects the `ochid:` trailers itself (the
`commit-app` / `commit-claude` stages append them) — don't
hand-write them into the commit body or `--title`/`--body`.

**Current limitation**: only fully supports the
[Keep separate](#shape-at-close-out-push) shape; other
shapes need manual jj steps. Improvements tracked /
planned:

- Merge non-ff close-out without the manual pre-commits
  (`## Todo` entry "vc-x1 push: pause point between commit
  and publish stages") — commit stages run normally, pause
  for the merge rebase, resume via `--from bookmark-set`.
- N:1 code↔bot for code worked outside vc-x1 (`## Todo`
  entry "vc-x1 push: record uncovered code commits").
- Symmetric squash — demoted to `todo-backlog.md`
  ("vc-x1 push --squash"): after-publication squash is
  off the routine path now that Merge non-ff is the
  routine shape.

Landed: per-repo bookmark names (0.68.0) — `<bookmark>` is
code-repo-only; the session repo is pinned to `main`
throughout push and sync.

### .claude cadence

**Cadence**: one push = one `.claude` commit, paired
with every code commit in that push.

The `.claude` working copy accumulates session data
across the cycle; its change ID stays stable across
snapshots, `jj describe`, and the squash-push fold, so
code-side `ochid:` trailers resolve.

`.claude` is a linear journal — all session work lives
on `main`, regardless of the app-side bookmark. **Do not
create or maintain `.claude` bookmarks that mirror
app-side branches** — risks the bot steering session
pushes to the wrong remote ref.

Ending a session: if the user runs `/exit` there will be
session information created, which we don't worry about.
The user can close the terminal instead and `@` will
remain empty — this behavior was verified in 0.64.0.

### Bot communication at the reviews

Use plain prose — no insider jargon ("Gate N signal",
"Checkpoint N", etc.):

- **At Work review** — summarize what changed and stop.
  "Work complete. Please review."
- **At Commit Description review** — present `$TITLE`
  and `$BODY` explicitly; ask permission to commit/push.
  Don't spell out the full `vc-x1 push ... --title ...
  --body ...` invocation by default.
- **At Post close-out review** — surface the shape
  options (squash / merge / keep) and the push target;
  wait for the user's choice before any `jj squash` /
  `jj rebase` / `jj git push` invocation.

### After push or squash-push: stop and wait

After a **push** (crossing the remote boundary, by hand or

input.content:
Rename `finalize` to `squash-push`: mechanism-named, repo-generic,
zero-ceremony (bare invocation squashes @ into @- and pushes main
in `.`). The detach machinery retires with it — the command runs
fully in-process, so failures are visible non-zero exits and the
failure-marker files have nothing left to report. Push's session
stage is renamed `squash-push-bot`, the first use of the work/bot
stage vocabulary. Protocol docs are rewritten for the inline world.

- src/finalize.rs -> src/squash_push.rs
  - SquashPushArgs/SquashPushParams: positional BOOKMARK
    (default main), -R/--repo (default .), --squash pair
    override (default @,@-)
  - retire --detach/--delay/--exec/--push and detach(),
    build_exec_args(), write_failure_marker(),
    surface_previous_failures(), status_dir()
  - finalize_inline dissolves: squash_push() (preflight +
    execute) is the single entry point
  - empty-@ feedback: bookmark at target and remote ->
    "already sync'd", exit 0; remote behind -> skip the
    squash, still push
  - tests rewritten for the new surface; a test pins that
    `finalize` no longer parses (no deprecated alias)
- src/push.rs: Stage::FinalizeClaude -> SquashPushBot
  ("squash-push-bot"), --no-finalize -> --no-squash-push,
  stage body calls squash_push::squash_push; doc updates
- src/push/tests.rs, src/push/integration_tests.rs: rename
  sweep; stage tests renamed push_squash_push_bot_*
- src/main.rs: Commands::SquashPush wiring + long_about;
  push help title made precise ("Commit both repos, push the
  work repo, squash-push the bot repo"); squash-push short
  help generic (SOURCE/TARGET); surface_previous_failures
  call removed
- src/context.rs: drop the log field and load(log) param —
  its only reader was the detach re-exec
- src/subcommand.rs, src/common.rs, src/test_helpers.rs:
  doc-comment updates for the rename and Context::load()
- tests/cli_sync.rs: --no-finalize -> --no-squash-push
- notes/cycle-protocol.md: stop-and-wait rewritten succinct
  and directive-scoped (intermediate pushes of a delegated
  task proceed; the hard stop lands on the turn's final
  push); Recovery recipe is now `vc-x1 squash-push -R
  .claude` + stale pre-0.69.0-2 state-file caveat
- notes/bugs.md: prune #3 (surface_previous_failures racy)
  as moot; renumber
- notes/todo.md: -2 marked done with decisions recorded;
  -4 rescoped (README, terminology + stage-name sweep); new
  Todos #9 (jj-query facade dedup), #10 (push/state.rs
  split + jj-lib vs subprocess rule), #11 (configurable
  bot-repo directory name)
- AGENTS.md: "finalize" as a plain word reworded to "seal"
  (collides with the retired command name)
- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: version 0.69.0-2

content.text:
That's the zero-ceremony path working exactly as designed — first manual use of the bare invocation. Reading your transcript:

- `@` held the session tail (my closing words + the push transcript), so it squashed `@` into `@-` rather than taking the empty-skip branch my in-push run hit.
- The chid stayed stable (`lsvwpwsy` before and after) while the commit id moved `2be995dd` → `02587418` — the fold rewrote the published bot commit and the push force-updated `main`, so the work-repo `ochid:` trailer still resolves.
- Ended clean: empty `@` on top of `main`, nothing left behind.

So the whole 0.69.0-2 surface got exercised live today: the in-push `squash-push-bot` stage (empty-`@` skip branch) and the hand-run tail fold (squash branch). Ready to pick up 0.69.0-3 (push preflight backstop for a `.claude main` ahead of `main@origin`) whenever you say.

file.content:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

**feat: inline session push + squash-push (0.69.0)**

Push's `finalize-claude` stage detaches a delayed child to
squash+push `.claude`; a sandboxed bot run kills the child at
command exit — silently, every cycle (see Bugs #1 [[24]]). The
empty-@ goal behind the detach/delay dance is self-referential
for the bot (finalizing is itself session data), so the bot
forgoes it; only the user, acting after the turn, can capture
the full tail.

Design notes:
- naming (decided 2026-07-15): `finalize` → `squash-push`
  - mechanism-named and repo-generic: a squash `@ → @-` +
    push
  - needed frequently on the bot repo (the session tail)
  - occasionally useful on the app repo: amend-and-push —
    a deliberate published-history rewrite + forced update
- squash-push of a just-pushed session commit rewrites it,
  so its push is a forced update
  - functions fine whether the bot or the user runs it
  - but on the bot repo only the user gets an empty `@`:
    the bot's own invocation writes new session data, so
    its `@` is non-empty again immediately
  - on any repo without a live writer it works as expected
    for anyone
- CLI shape
  - `-R`/`--repo`, default `.` (house convention: `-R`
    repo, `-r` revision)
  - positional BOOKMARK defaulting to `main` (mirrors push)
  - repo stays a flag — no command takes a positional repo
- no-op feedback
  - `@` empty and bookmark already at remote → "repo is
    already sync'd with remote", exit 0
  - `@` empty but remote behind → skip the squash, still
    push
- the bot may also run `squash-push` as a separate later
  command to capture the `vc-x1 push` record (leaves only
  its own)

Plan:
- 0.69.0-0 prep: backfill Commits:, bump version, pick up
  Todo #1, open chores section (done)
- 0.69.0-1 push: session squash+push inline (done)
  - replace the finalize-claude shell-out with an
    in-process squash of the trailing session writes +
    `jj git push --bookmark main -R .claude`
  - a session-push failure is a visible push failure
  - crate temporarily named `vc-x1-dev` so per-commit
    installs don't clobber the stable `vc-x1` another bot
    instance uses
  - tests
- 0.69.0-2 squash-push: rename `finalize` → `squash-push` (done)
  - zero-ceremony default: `-R .`, BOOKMARK positional
    defaulting to `main`, no-op feedback
  - retire `--detach` / `--delay`
  - module + types follow: `finalize.rs` →
    `squash_push.rs`, `FinalizeArgs` / `FinalizeParams` →
    `SquashPushArgs` / `SquashPushParams`
  - `finalize_inline` dissolves: with the detach branch
    gone, preflight + exec is the command's only path, so
    the shim merges into the renamed entry point

content.content:
80	    the shim merges into the renamed entry point
81	  - failure-marker machinery (decided at -2): retired
82	    entirely with `--detach` — markers only existed for the
83	    detached child's invisible exit; any stale ones under
84	    `~/.cache/vc-x1/finalize-status` are inert (delete by
85	    hand)
86	  - rename push stage `finalize-claude` →
87	    `squash-push-bot` (first use of the work/bot stage
88	    vocabulary; the remaining app/claude stages sweep at
89	    -4); `--no-finalize` → `--no-squash-push`
90	  - deprecated `finalize` alias (decided at -2): none —
91	    the flag surface changed incompatibly, so an alias
92	    would trade a clear "unrecognized subcommand" for
93	    confusing flag errors
94	  - `Context.log` follows `--detach` out: its only reader
95	    was the detach re-exec, so the field and `load(log)`
96	    parameter are gone
97	  - tests
98	  - docs pulled forward from -4: cycle-protocol.md
99	    stop-and-wait + Recovery rewritten for the inline
100	    world; AGENTS.md scrubbed of "finalize" as a plain
101	    word (collides with the retired command name)
102	- 0.69.0-3 push preflight backstop: error (or auto-push)
103	  when `.claude main` is ahead of `main@origin`; tests
104	- 0.69.0-4 docs: README (cycle-protocol's stop-and-wait +
105	  Recovery sections were rewritten at -2); repo-terminology
106	  sweep — "bot repo" / "work repo" (decided 2026-07-15)
107	  replace "session repo" / "code repo" / "app repo" across
108	  AGENTS.md, cycle-protocol.md, and code doc comments; push
109	  stage-name sweep to match (`commit-app` → `commit-work`,
110	  `commit-claude` → `commit-bot`, `push-app` → `push-work`;
111	  `squash-push-bot` landed at -2)
112	- 0.69.0 close-out and validation
113	
114	Continuity (resume 2026-07-15):
115	- next: pick up -2 (mark it `(current)`) — the squash-push
116	  rename; every decision needed is in Design notes above
117	- installs this cycle: `cargo install --path . --locked`
118	  lands `vc-x1-dev`; PATH `vc-x1` stays 0.69.0-0 for the
119	  other bot instance; rename the crate back to `vc-x1` when
120	  that window closes (decide at close-out)
121	- cycle pushes go straight to `main` (keep-separate shape;
122	  -0 and -1 already published)
123	- -1 was pushed with `vc-x1-dev push` — first dogfood of
124	  the inline session push; the bot sandbox now allows
125	  `~/.config/jj`, `/tmp/`, and `~/.cargo` writes, so tests
126	  and `cargo install` run sandboxed (fixed 2026-07-15)
127	
128	## Todo
129	
130	 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
131	 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
132	 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md` to renumber.
133	 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
134	 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
135	 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
136	 number. Long-tail entries
137	 live in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Use the
138	 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](/AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
139	 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
140	 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).
141	
142	1. **vc-x1 push: pause point between commit and publish
143	   stages.** The merge non-ff close-out is a three-step
144	   sequence:
145	   - commit the close-out pair locally (normal 1:1 commit
146	     stages, ochids injected both directions)
147	   - rebase the code-side close-out into the merge (chids
148	     survive rebase, so every ochid stays valid)
149	   - publish

file.content:
the shim merges into the renamed entry point
  - failure-marker machinery (decided at -2): retired
    entirely with `--detach` — markers only existed for the
    detached child's invisible exit; any stale ones under
    `~/.cache/vc-x1/finalize-status` are inert (delete by
    hand)
  - rename push stage `finalize-claude` →
    `squash-push-bot` (first use of the work/bot stage
    vocabulary; the remaining app/claude stages sweep at
    -4); `--no-finalize` → `--no-squash-push`
  - deprecated `finalize` alias (decided at -2): none —
    the flag surface changed incompatibly, so an alias
    would trade a clear "unrecognized subcommand" for
    confusing flag errors
  - `Context.log` follows `--detach` out: its only reader
    was the detach re-exec, so the field and `load(log)`
    parameter are gone
  - tests
  - docs pulled forward from -4: cycle-protocol.md
    stop-and-wait + Recovery rewritten for the inline
    world; AGENTS.md scrubbed of "finalize" as a plain
    word (collides with the retired command name)
- 0.69.0-3 push preflight backstop: error (or auto-push)
  when `.claude main` is ahead of `main@origin`; tests
- 0.69.0-4 docs: README (cycle-protocol's stop-and-wait +
  Recovery sections were rewritten at -2); repo-terminology
  sweep — "bot repo" / "work repo" (decided 2026-07-15)
  replace "session repo" / "code repo" / "app repo" across
  AGENTS.md, cycle-protocol.md, and code doc comments; push
  stage-name sweep to match (`commit-app` → `commit-work`,
  `commit-claude` → `commit-bot`, `push-app` → `push-work`;
  `squash-push-bot` landed at -2)
- 0.69.0 close-out and validation

Continuity (resume 2026-07-15):
- next: pick up -2 (mark it `(current)`) — the squash-push
  rename; every decision needed is in Design notes above
- installs this cycle: `cargo install --path . --locked`
  lands `vc-x1-dev`; PATH `vc-x1` stays 0.69.0-0 for the
  other bot instance; rename the crate back to `vc-x1` when
  that window closes (decide at close-out)
- cycle pushes go straight to `main` (keep-separate shape;
  -0 and -1 already published)
- -1 was pushed with `vc-x1-dev push` — first dogfood of
  the inline session push; the bot sandbox now allows
  `~/.config/jj`, `/tmp/`, and `~/.cargo` writes, so tests
  and `cargo install` run sandboxed (fixed 2026-07-15)

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](/AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **vc-x1 push: pause point between commit and publish
   stages.** The merge non-ff close-out is a three-step
   sequence:
   - commit the close-out pair locally (normal 1:1 commit
     stages, ochids injected both directions)
   - rebase the code-side close-out into the merge (chids
     survive rebase, so every ochid stays valid)
   - publish
