input.description: Read the 0.68.1 diagnosis section

content.content:
## docs: diagnose silent session-push loss (0.68.1)

Commits: [[24]]

Bugs #1 reported the session repo "sometimes not pushed"
(tprobe: GitHub bot repo 8 commits behind while both local
repos and the GitHub code repo agreed). Diagnosed 2026-07-14;
this cycle records the diagnosis and queues the fix design as
Todo #1. Single-commit cycle.

- Root cause: push's `finalize-claude` stage delegates the
  session push to a detached (`setsid`) child that sleeps 10s
  first; a sandboxed Bash command kills its pid namespace at
  command exit, so a bot-run push loses the child every time —
  before its first `jj` command.
- Evidence: tprobe app repo has 8 `push bookmark main` ops;
  its `.claude` has zero squash/push ops since clone;
  reproduced with a bare `setsid` child in this project's
  sandbox.
- Silent by construction:
  - SIGKILL leaves no failure marker
  - `~/.cache/vc-x1` is outside the sandbox write allowlist
    and `write_failure_marker` swallows errors by design
  - `verify_completion_sanity` checks local state only
- Fix design (Todo #1): push does everything inline (squash
  micro-tail + `jj git push` in-process); preflight errors
  when `.claude main` is ahead of `main@origin`; `finalize`
  becomes the user's zero-arg empty-@ tidy-up, run after the
  bot goes quiet. The empty-@ goal is self-referential for
  the bot — finalizing is itself session data — so only the
  user can capture the full tail.

### As-built ladder

- 0.68.1 single-commit cycle: bugs.md diagnosis rewrite,
  Todo #1 fix design, Done entry, 0.68.0 `Commits:`
  backfill, Done 0.51.0–0.65.2 batch retired to done.md
  (refs re-slotted to [96]..[112] — todo.md's numbers
  collided with done.md's namespace), version bump + this
  section.

## feat: inline session push + squash-push (0.69.0)

questions.question: When push preflight finds `.claude main` not synced with `main@origin` (ahead, or diverged from a failed squash-push fold), what should it do?

options.label: Warn + auto-push (Recommended)
options.description: Preflight logs a loud "bot repo main was not published — pushing now" and runs the push itself. Restores visibility without friction; the end stage would publish the same commits anyway, so erroring protects nothing. Closes the window even if this push later aborts mid-way.

options.label: Error, user resolves
options.description: As written in the 0.68.1 fix design: preflight errors; user runs `vc-x1 squash-push -R .claude` (or jj git push) and reruns push. Maximum visibility, maximum friction; resolution is exactly what the tool could do itself.

options.label: Warn only, continue
options.description: Preflight logs the warning but pushes nothing; squash-push-bot publishes at the end as it already does. Least code, but a push that aborts before the last stage leaves the commits unpublished.

content.text: What would you like to clarify about the backstop options?

message.content: I actually don't understand the problem, main is generally aways ahead of main@origin when does this come into play?

content.text:
Good question — the answer is jj-specific: in this workflow `.claude main` is normally **never** ahead of `main@origin` between pushes.

In git, local `main` advances every time you commit, so ahead-of-origin is the everyday state. But in the bot repo here, ongoing session work accrues in `@` (the working copy commit), which no bookmark points at. `main` only moves at one place: push's `bookmark-set` stage (or `squash-push`'s `jj bookmark set`), and in both cases the very next step pushes it. So at the start of any push, `main` and `main@origin` should be **identical** — the bookmark move and its publish are supposed to be one atomic-ish pair.

When they disagree at push start, it means that pair was broken: a previous run moved the bookmark but the publish never happened. That's exactly the silent-loss bug — the old detached `finalize-claude` child was killed after `bookmark-set` but before its `jj git push`, every cycle, which is how tprobe's GitHub bot repo ended up 8 commits behind while every local check looked fine (`verify_completion_sanity` only checked local state, where main *had* moved). The other way to get there today: a manual `vc-x1 squash-push` that fails at the network step — bookmark already moved to the folded commit, push didn't land, leaving local main diverged from origin.

So the backstop is a tripwire for "a previous publish was lost": at push start, if `.claude main` ≠ `main@origin`, something upstream failed silently, and the question is whether preflight should error (you investigate and push by hand), warn + auto-push (heal it and continue), or just warn and let the final `squash-push-bot` stage publish it anyway as it naturally would.

Does that frame it? If so — which behavior do you want?

message.content: How/when will you be checking for this condition?

content.text:
In `stage_preflight` — the first stage of every `vc-x1 push` run, right after the existing `verify_tracking` calls and before the sync/cargo steps. Concretely:

- Resolve the `.claude` repo's `main` and `main@origin` to commit ids (same `jj log -T commit_id` lookup `squash_push.rs` already uses for its "already sync'd" check).
- If they're equal — the normal state — nothing happens, preflight continues.
- If they differ, the backstop fires with whichever behavior we pick (error / auto-push / warn).

Timing caveats:

- It runs once per push invocation, at the start. A resumed push (`--from <stage>`) skips preflight by default (`--re-preflight` forces it), so the check is effectively "on every fresh push".
- It's a preflight-time snapshot, not continuous monitoring — a publish lost *during* this push (say the final stage's network push fails) is caught by that stage's own visible error now that everything is inline, and by this backstop at the *next* push if it somehow went unnoticed.
- Scope is the bot repo only. The work repo's `push-app` failure was always loud and inline, so it doesn't need a tripwire; the plan line scopes the backstop to `.claude`.

So: at every fresh push start, "did the last session publish actually land?" — and if not, error, auto-push, or warn-and-continue. Which do you want?

lastPrompt: How/when will you be checking for this condition?

message.content: Seems to me it should be checked more often, like every "{re}acquaint, Nth command, every 10min and be configurable?

content.text:
Worth pulling apart, because the condition has one important property: it can only *become* wrong at a publish event. Between pushes, nothing moves `.claude main` — the state doesn't drift on its own. So checking every 10 minutes doesn't detect new damage; it re-detects the same stale fact sooner than the next natural checkpoint. That said, "sooner" has real value — the whole bug was that the damage sat invisible for 8 cycles — and the check is two `jj log` lookups, cheap enough to run anywhere. The pieces on the table:

- **Shared check function** — resolve `main` vs `main@origin` in the bot repo, report equal/ahead/diverged. Everything below is a call site of this one function.
- **Push preflight** (the -3 plan) — the highest-value site, since a push is about to stack more work on the unpublished state. The error/auto-push/warn choice applies here.
- **Every vc-x1 invocation** — a cheap startup hook on subcommands, gated by `.vc-config.toml` (e.g. `bot_sync_check = warn|error|off`). This is your "every Nth command" made simple: N=1 costs ~two subprocess spawns.
- **Reacquaint** — a protocol edit, not code: AGENTS.md's acquaint routine gains "run the check" (whatever command exposes it — could be `vc-x1 squash-push`-adjacent, a `--check` flag, or the invocation hook making it automatic).
- **Every 10 min** — vc-x1 has no resident process, so a builtin timer means a daemon, which I'd avoid. But a standalone check command + a user-side systemd timer gets the same effect with zero new architecture.

My recommendation: -3 lands the shared function + the preflight backstop + a read-only way to invoke the check standalone; the config-gated every-invocation hook and the AGENTS.md reacquaint edit are small enough to fold in too if you want them now. The systemd-timer route needs nothing from us beyond the standalone command.

message.content: I suggest there is a command `vc-x1 validate-bot` and also `vc-x1 squash-push`, `vc-x1 push` at a minimum check it.

content.text:
`vc-x1 validate-bot` it is — fits the existing `validate-todo` / `validate-repo` family, read-only, exits non-zero when the bot repo's `main` ≠ `main@origin` (usable standalone, from reacquaint, or a timer). And `push` + `squash-push` both run the check at start.

One note on `squash-push`: publishing is its whole job, so when it finds the condition it heals it by proceeding — the check there is about *saying so* ("bot repo main was not published by a previous run") rather than changing behavior. The open choice is really just `push` preflight:

content.text: [Request interrupted by user]

message.content: Let me be sure my understanding is correct, the bot repo is in an unexpected state if main != main@origin

content.text:
Correct — with one refinement: the invariant holds **at rest**. Two states to distinguish:

- **Mid-command (expected mismatch):** inside a running `push` or `squash-push`, between its `jj bookmark set` and its `jj git push`, local `main` is legitimately ahead (or diverged, when a fold rewrote the published commit). That window lives entirely inside one command invocation — seconds.
- **At rest (unexpected mismatch):** any time no push is in flight, `main != main@origin` means a previous run moved the bookmark but its publish never landed — a lost push (the old detached-child kill, a network failure at the push step, an interrupted command).

Since `validate-bot` and the preflight check only ever run *between* commands, anything they see is by definition the at-rest case: mismatch = something upstream failed. The check never races the legitimate window (a `vc-x1 push` isn't running concurrently with its own preflight, and the bot/user don't run two pushes at once).

One boundary case for `validate-bot` to define: `main@origin` not existing at all (a bot repo that has never been pushed). I'd report that as its own distinct error ("bookmark 'main' has never been pushed to origin") rather than folding it into "mismatch" — same exit code, clearer message.

message.content: Ok, so if determing that is cheap a "validate-bot" could be one of the first thing `vc-x1` does on every execution. Note; could not saying should!
Ok, so if "validate-bot" is cheap it could be the first thing `vc-x1` does on every execution. Note; could not saying should!

content.text:
Noted as a *could*. It is cheap — the whole check can be a single jj spawn (`jj bookmark list main --all-remotes` reports the local and `@origin` positions in one call), so ~tens of milliseconds. The wrinkles that make it a design decision rather than a freebie:

- **Locating the bot repo** — the hook only makes sense when an invocation can find one (`<cwd>/.claude` or via config); commands run elsewhere would silently skip.
- **Severity for unrelated commands** — `vc-x1 fix-todo` erroring because a session push was lost would be obnoxious; the hook probably *warns* on stderr while `push`/`squash-push`/`validate-bot` are the ones that error. That's the `warn|error|off` config knob.
- It also ties into Todo #11 (configurable bot-repo directory name) — the hook's "find the bot repo" logic is the same lookup.

I'll capture that as a Todo ("run validate-bot at every vc-x1 invocation, config-gated") and keep -3 to the agreed minimum: `validate-bot` subcommand + `push` preflight errors on mismatch + `squash-push` detects-and-reports. Formalizing the -3 plan in todo.md now, then implementing.

file.content:
11. **Make the bot repo directory name configurable (not
    hardcoded `.claude`).** The bot repo lives at `.claude`
    because that is where Claude Code keeps session data,
    but the concept is agent-agnostic and the user wants to
    change it (decided 2026-07-15: the name does not need to
    be `.claude`). Today the literal is embedded across
    init/clone/push/sync, `claude_path()`, ochid path
    semantics (`/.claude/<chid>`), the
    `~/.claude/projects/<path>` symlink, and docs.
    - `.vc-config.toml` already records each repo's
      workspace path — likely the natural home for the
      configurable name.
    - Open: migration for existing workspaces, the ochid
      trailer prefix (recorded in immutable history), and
      the Claude Code symlink whose location the harness
      controls.
12. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**
    `0.47.0` introduced the convention (single-field leaf names
    its field `value`, declares the flag via `#[arg(long = "…")]`,
    so consumers read `args.<leaf>.value` not `args.<leaf>.<leaf>`)
    on the new `squash` leaf. Sweep the pre-existing single-field
    leaves to match: `repo`, `dry_run`, `private`, `account`,
    `config`, `use_template` + their consumers
    (`init.rs`, tests).

    Note: can a single field be defined as an type or enum instead
    of a struct and maybe eliminate the `args.<leaf>.<leaf>` name
    issue.
13. **`por → dual` conversion.** Attach a `.claude`
    companion repo + `.vc-config.toml` to an existing por

      warn at most; push / squash-push / validate-bot
      already have their own handling from 0.69.0-3

1	//! Integration tests for the push module.
2	//!
3	//! End-to-end tests for `push_in` against real dual-repo jj
4	//! fixtures (bare-git remotes + colocated jj repos under a
5	//! unique tempdir via `crate::test_helpers::Fixture`).
6	//!
7	//! Every test uses `--from message` to skip `preflight` (no
8	//! `Cargo.toml` in the fixture). Most also use `--no-squash-push`
9	//! to focus on the earlier stages (message, commit-app,
10	//! commit-claude, bookmark-set, push-app); the
11	//! `push_squash_push_bot_*` tests run the in-process
12	//! `squash-push-bot` stage for real. Everything is exercised
13	//! against the fixture's local bare-git remotes.
14	//!
15	//! Stage execution + rollback are covered here;
16	//! state-file / layout / stage-ordering mechanics are covered
17	//! in the neighboring `tests` module via pure unit tests.
18	//!
19	//! Requires `jj` and the compiled `vc-x1` binary in `PATH`.
20	
21	use super::*;
22	use crate::test_helpers::Fixture;
23	use std::fs;
24	use std::process::Command;
25	
26	/// Run `jj <args> -R <repo>` and return trimmed stdout on success.
27	fn jj(repo: &Path, args: &[&str]) -> String {
28	    let out = Command::new("jj")
29	        .args(args)
30	        .arg("-R")
31	        .arg(repo)
32	        .output()
33	        .expect("spawn jj");
34	    assert!(
35	        out.status.success(),
36	        "jj {args:?} failed in {}: {}",
37	        repo.display(),
38	        String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr)
39	    );
40	    String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stdout).trim().to_string()
41	}
42	
43	/// Commit ID (short, 12 chars) for a revision.
44	fn cid(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> String {
45	    jj(
46	        repo,
47	        &["log", "-r", rev, "--no-graph", "-T", "commit_id.short(12)"],
48	    )
49	}
50	
51	/// Full description of a revision.
52	fn description(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> String {
53	    jj(repo, &["log", "-r", rev, "--no-graph", "-T", "description"])
54	}
55	
56	/// First line of a revision's description.
57	fn desc_first_line(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> String {
58	    jj(
59	        repo,
60	        &[
61	            "log",
62	            "-r",
63	            rev,
64	            "--no-graph",
65	            "-T",
66	            "description.first_line()",
67	        ],
68	    )
69	}
70	
71	/// Standard test params: bookmark=main, `--from message` (skip
72	/// preflight), `--no-squash-push` (skip the session squash+push),
73	/// `--yes` (auto-approve any interactive prompts).
74	fn test_params(title: &str, body: &str) -> PushParams {
75	    PushParams {
76	        bookmark: Some("main".to_string()),
77	        restart: false,
78	        from: Some(Stage::Message),
79	        step: false,
80	        status: false,
81	        recheck: false,
82	        no_squash_push: true,
83	        dry_run: false,
84	        title: Some(title.to_string()),
85	        body: Some(body.to_string()),
86	        yes: true,
87	    }
88	}
89	
90	/// Happy path when `.claude` has no pending changes: the app
91	/// commit lands with an `ochid` trailer pointing at `.claude`'s
92	/// pre-existing `@-`, `commit-claude` is skipped, and both
93	/// `bookmark-set` + `push-app` still run cleanly.
94	#[test]
95	fn push_happy_claude_clean() {
96	    let fx = Fixture::new("push-clean");
97	    fs::write(fx.work.join("hello.txt"), "hi").expect("write app file");
98	
99	    let claude_main_before = cid(&fx.claude, "main");
100	
101	    push_in(&fx.work, &test_params("feat: clean case", "app body")).expect("push should succeed");
102	
103	    // App repo: main advanced to our new commit.
104	    assert_eq!(desc_first_line(&fx.work, "main"), "feat: clean case");
105	    let app_full = description(&fx.work, "main");
106	    assert!(
107	        app_full.contains("ochid: /.claude/"),
108	        "app ochid trailer missing:\n{app_full}"
109	    );
110	
111	    // `.claude` main unchanged (no commit happened there).
112	    assert_eq!(
113	        cid(&fx.claude, "main"),
114	        claude_main_before,
115	        ".claude main should not have moved"
116	    );
117	}

122	fn push_happy_claude_dirty() {

135	
136	    // Cross-repo ochid trailers are both present.
137	    let app_full = description(&fx.work, "main");

140	        app_full.contains("ochid: /.claude/"),

151	
152	    // `.claude` main moved off its initial commit.
153	    assert_ne!(
154	        cid(&fx.claude, "main"),
155	        claude_main_before,
156	        ".claude main should have advanced"
157	    );
158	}

1	//! `push` subcommand — collapse the dual-repo commit+push+
2	//! squash-push ceremony into a single resumable command.
3	//!
4	//! See `notes/chores/chores-05.md > Add push subcommand (0.37.0)` for the
5	//! full design.
6	//!
7	//! Dev-step ladder (expanded from original 4 to 6 after adding an
8	//! integration-test step ahead of the first dogfood):
9	//!
10	//! - `0.37.0-0` — scaffolding: flag surface, `Stage` enum, stub `push()`
11	//! - `0.37.0-1` — state file + stage-dispatch loop with stage stubs;
12	//!   `--status`, `--restart`, `--from`
13	//! - `0.37.0-2` — real stage bodies (commits, bookmarks, push,
14	//!   finalize) + `jj op` snapshot rollback
15	//! - `0.37.0-3` — integration tests + workspace-root refactor
16	//!   (thread `root: &Path` through every stage so fixtures can
17	//!   point them at tempdirs); first `vc-x1 push` dogfood ships
18	//!   this commit
19	//! - `0.37.0-4` — interactivity: two approval gates, `$EDITOR`,
20	//!   message persistence across resumes
21	//! - `0.37.0-5` — polish: `--dry-run`, `--step`, non-tty handling,
22	//!   `.gitignore` coherence warning
23	//! - `0.37.0` — docs + workflow migration (done marker)
24	
25	use std::fs;
26	use std::io::IsTerminal;
27	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
28	
29	use chrono::Utc;
30	use clap::{Args, ValueEnum};
31	use log::{debug, info, warn};
32	
33	use crate::common::{prompt, run};
34	use crate::context::Context;
35	use crate::options_flags::squash::SquashSpec;
36	use crate::subcommand::SubcommandRunner;
37	use crate::sync::{current_op_id, op_restore};
38	use crate::toml_simple::toml_load;
39	
40	/// Bookmark the session (`.claude`) repo always advances and
41	/// pushes. The session repo is a linear journal on `main` by
42	/// design — `<bookmark>` names a code-repo bookmark only.
43	const SESSION_BOOKMARK: &str = "main";
44	
45	/// Named stages of the `push` state machine.
46	///
47	/// Used by `--from <stage>` to resume at a specific point and by
48	/// `--status` to report the current position. Ordered top-down so
49	/// `Stage as u8` comparisons reflect progress through the flow.
50	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, ValueEnum)]
51	#[value(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
52	pub enum Stage {
53	    /// Run fmt / clippy / test / install / retest.
54	    Preflight,
55	    /// Present diff for the first approval gate.
56	    Review,
57	    /// Compose / edit the commit message; present for second gate.
58	    Message,
59	    /// Commit the app repo.
60	    CommitApp,
61	    /// Commit the `.claude` session repo (skipped if empty).
62	    CommitClaude,
63	    /// Advance each repo's bookmark to its `@-`: app → `<bookmark>`,
64	    /// session → `main`.
65	    BookmarkSet,
66	    /// `jj git push --bookmark <b> -R .`.
67	    PushApp,
68	    /// In-process squash of `.claude`'s trailing session writes
69	    /// + push `main`.
70	    SquashPushBot,
71	}
72	
73	impl Stage {
74	    /// Return the stage's kebab-case string identifier (matches what
75	    /// the CLI accepts via `--from <stage>` and what `PushState`
76	    /// persists to disk).
77	    pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
78	        match self {
79	            Stage::Preflight => "preflight",
80	            Stage::Review => "review",
81	            Stage::Message => "message",
82	            Stage::CommitApp => "commit-app",
83	            Stage::CommitClaude => "commit-claude",
84	            Stage::BookmarkSet => "bookmark-set",
85	            Stage::PushApp => "push-app",
86	            Stage::SquashPushBot => "squash-push-bot",
87	        }
88	    }
89	
90	    /// Parse a kebab-case stage name back into a `Stage`.
91	    ///
92	    /// Unknown names return `None`; callers should surface a helpful
93	    /// error rather than silently substituting a default.
94	    pub fn from_str(name: &str) -> Option<Self> {
95	        match name {
96	            "preflight" => Some(Stage::Preflight),
97	            "review" => Some(Stage::Review),
98	            "message" => Some(Stage::Message),
99	            "commit-app" => Some(Stage::CommitApp),
100	            "commit-claude" => Some(Stage::CommitClaude),
101	            "bookmark-set" => Some(Stage::BookmarkSet),
102	            "push-app" => Some(Stage::PushApp),
103	            "squash-push-bot" => Some(Stage::SquashPushBot),
104	            _ => None,
105	        }
106	    }
107	
108	    /// The stage that follows this one, or `None` when this is the
109	    /// last stage (`SquashPushBot`).
110	    pub fn next(self) -> Option<Self> {
111	        match self {
112	            Stage::Preflight => Some(Stage::Review),
113	            Stage::Review => Some(Stage::Message),
114	            Stage::Message => Some(Stage::CommitApp),
115	            Stage::CommitApp => Some(Stage::CommitClaude),
116	            Stage::CommitClaude => Some(Stage::BookmarkSet),
117	            Stage::BookmarkSet => Some(Stage::PushApp),
118	            Stage::PushApp => Some(Stage::SquashPushBot),
119	            Stage::SquashPushBot => None,
120	        }
121	    }
122	
123	    /// The first stage in the flow — used when no saved state exists.
124	    pub fn first() -> Self {
125	        Stage::Preflight
126	    }
127	}
128	
129	/// CLI arguments for the `push` subcommand.
130	///
131	/// Flag set mirrors the design in `notes/chores/chores-05.md`. Flags are
132	/// parsed in 0.37.0-0; their real effects land across the remaining
133	/// 0.37.0-N steps. See the module docstring for which flags activate
134	/// when.
135	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
136	pub struct PushArgs {
137	    /// Bookmark to advance in the code repo (positional form of
138	    /// `--bookmark`). The session repo always advances `main`.
139	    ///
140	    /// Accepting a positional lets the common case read as `vc-x1 push main`
141	    /// without the `--bookmark` ceremony; `--bookmark` is kept as an
142	    /// alias for scripts and for composition with other args. The two
143	    /// forms conflict if both supplied.
144	    #[arg(value_name = "BOOKMARK", conflicts_with = "bookmark")]
145	    pub bookmark_pos: Option<String>,
146	
147	    /// Bookmark to advance in the code repo (flag form; see positional).
148	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "bookmark_pos")]
149	    pub bookmark: Option<String>,
150	
151	    /// Clear any saved state file and start from stage 1.
152	    #[arg(long)]
153	    pub restart: bool,
154	
155	    /// Explicit stage to jump to (advanced / debug use).
156	    #[arg(long, value_name = "STAGE")]
157	    pub from: Option<Stage>,
158	
159	    /// Pause between every stage for an interactive approval gate.
160	    #[arg(long)]
161	    pub step: bool,
162	
163	    /// Print where the saved state thinks we are and exit.
164	    #[arg(long)]
165	    pub status: bool,
166	
167	    /// Re-run `preflight` even on resume (default: skip if last run succeeded).
168	    #[arg(long)]
169	    pub recheck: bool,
170	
171	    /// Stop before `squash-push-bot` so it can be run manually.
172	    #[arg(long)]
173	    pub no_squash_push: bool,
174	
175	    /// Print the exact commands for every stage without side effects.
176	    #[arg(long)]
177	    pub dry_run: bool,
178	
179	    /// Commit title (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage).
180	    #[arg(long, value_name = "STR")]
181	    pub title: Option<String>,
182	
183	    /// Commit body (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage).
184	    #[arg(long, value_name = "STR")]
185	    pub body: Option<String>,
186	
187	    /// Auto-approve interactive prompts (review gate, message-edit
188	    /// confirmation). Required when `--title` / `--body` aren't both
189	    /// supplied in non-interactive contexts.
190	    #[arg(short = 'y', long)]
191	    pub yes: bool,
192	}
193	
194	/// Inputs to the push op, flat, owned, clap-free.
195	///
196	/// Mirrors `PushArgs`, with the two clap bookmark spellings
197	/// (positional `BOOKMARK` and `--bookmark`) collapsed into one
198	/// `bookmark` field. The rest map straight over: `restart`, `from`
199	/// (a `Stage`, a domain type — kept), `step`, `status`, `recheck`,
200	/// `no_squash_push`, `dry_run`, `title`, `body`, `yes`.
201	pub struct PushParams {
202	    pub bookmark: Option<String>,

input.old_string:
- 0.69.0-3 bot-repo published backstop (current)
  - invariant (clarified 2026-07-15): at rest the bot
    repo's `main == main@origin`; the bookmark only moves
    inside a push / squash-push run, which publishes it in
    the same invocation — an at-rest mismatch means a
    previous publish was lost (or never happened)
  - new `vc-x1 validate-bot`: read-only check of that
    invariant; distinct message when `main@origin` doesn't
    exist (never pushed); non-zero exit on any mismatch
  - push preflight runs the check and errors (decided
    2026-07-15: no automatic fixing) — resolve with
    `vc-x1 squash-push -R .claude`, rerun push
  - squash-push detects the same condition and reports it
    ("a previous publish was lost"), then proceeds —
    publishing is its job, so healing is not auto-fixing
  - tests

- 0.69.0-3 bot-repo published backstop (done)
  - invariant (clarified 2026-07-15): at rest the bot
    repo's `main == main@origin`; the bookmark only moves
    inside a push / squash-push run, which publishes it in
    the same invocation — an at-rest mismatch means a
    previous publish was lost (or never happened)
  - new `vc-x1 validate-bot` (name chosen 2026-07-15):
    read-only check of that invariant + tracking; distinct
    message when `main@origin` doesn't exist (never
    pushed); non-zero exit on any finding; no cargo steps
    — cheap enough for routine use (reacquaint, timers)

  - shared machinery in common.rs: `PublishState` +
    `bookmark_publish_state` + `verify_bot_published`
  - tests; new `test_helpers::jj_ok` for new tests instead
    of a 4th hand-rolled jj helper copy (migrating the old
    copies stays with the jj-facade Todo)
  - the "run at every vc-x1 invocation, config-gated" idea
    is queued as its own Todo (a could, not a should)

Work complete. Please review. Summary of 0.69.0-3:

**New `vc-x1 validate-bot`** (`src/validate_bot.rs`) — read-only check that the bot repo is in its at-rest state: `main`'s remote refs tracked and `main` matching `main@origin`. Distinct messages for mismatch ("an earlier publish was likely lost") and never-pushed; non-zero exit on any finding; fixes nothing; no cargo steps — two jj lookups, cheap enough for reacquaint/timer use. `-R` defaults to `.claude`. Verified live on this workspace: reports `main is published at origin`.

**Shared machinery** (`src/common.rs`) — `PublishState` enum (`InSync` / `NeverPushed` / `Mismatch`) + `bookmark_publish_state()` (uses `present(...)` so a missing remote bookmark is a state, not an error) + `verify_bot_published()` (the error-raising wrapper naming the `vc-x1 squash-push` resolution).

**Push preflight** (`src/push.rs`) — runs the check immediately after `verify_tracking`, *before* the sync and cargo steps, so a lost publish fails fast without burning a cargo cycle. Errors, per your decision — no automatic fixing.

**squash-push** (`src/squash_push.rs`) — detects the same condition at start and reports it (warn for mismatch, info for never-pushed), then proceeds: publishing is its job, so healing there is not auto-fixing.

**Tests** — 13 new, all suites green (406 unit + 5 + 4 + 5 + 6): parse tests for validate-bot; publish-state tests (in-sync / mismatch / never-pushed / missing bookmark); validate-bot ok + error paths; a push integration test proving preflight errors on an unpublished `.claude main`; a squash-push test proving a lost publish is healed and reported. New tests use a promoted `test_helpers::jj_ok` instead of a fourth hand-rolled jj helper copy (migrating the older copies stays with the jj-facade Todo).

**Notes** — the -3 ladder entry records the invariant and all decisions; new Todo #12 captures your "could run at every vc-x1 invocation" idea (config-gated, warn|error|off); the 0.69.0 chores `Commits:` line is backfilled with -0/-1/-2. `vc-x1-dev` 0.69.0-3 is installed.

message.content: Why in push.rs at line 882 do you have rust cargo preflight?

content.text:
That cargo preflight isn't new — it has been part of `push`'s `preflight` stage since the command was built (0.37.0). What I added at -3 is only lines 916–923, the bot-published check; the doc comment at 881 got reworded to mention the new check ahead of the pre-existing steps.

The original rationale (per the doc comment and chores-05 design): `vc-x1 push` wraps the whole per-commit flow, and AGENTS.md's pre-commit checklist requires `cargo fmt` / `clippy -D warnings` / `cargo test` before any commit — so push runs them itself rather than trusting that the bot/user already did. It's the "don't publish unchecked work" gate.

That said, your question points at a real wart: those steps are hardcoded and Rust-specific. `vc-x1 push` on a non-Rust project fails at preflight — the test fixtures dodge it with `--from review` precisely because they have no `Cargo.toml` (the fixture module doc says so), and `cli_sync.rs` does the same. So the tool currently assumes every workspace it pushes is a Rust project, which conflicts with vc-x1's otherwise repo-generic design.

If you want, I can add a Todo along the lines of "push preflight: make the build/test checks project-aware or configurable" — e.g. skip when no `Cargo.toml`, or read the check commands from `.vc-config.toml`. Want me to queue that? (It's out of scope for -3 — the -3 work itself is done and green.)

message.content: But vc-x1 should not assume anything about the contents of a repo other than looking for .vc-config and .jj, I think?

content.text:
Agreed — that's the right principle. vc-x1's contract with a repo is version control: `.jj` (the repo itself) and `.vc-config.toml` (the workspace metadata it wrote at init/clone). Everything else — language, build system, whether there's anything to "build" at all — is the repo's business. The hardcoded `cargo fmt/clippy/test` in push's preflight is the one place the tool reaches past that boundary and assumes "this is a Rust project"; the fixtures having to dodge their own tool's preflight (`--from review`, "no Cargo.toml in the fixture") is the tell.

The clean fix follows from the principle: the *repo* declares its checks, vc-x1 just runs them. `.vc-config.toml` grows an optional key — e.g. a `[preflight] checks = ["cargo fmt", "cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings", "cargo test"]` list — and push's preflight runs whatever is declared, nothing when absent. This project would declare its cargo cycle there, so the per-commit protocol stays enforced here while the tool becomes content-agnostic; fixtures and non-Rust projects get a preflight that's just the jj checks (tracking, bot-published, sync). Queuing it as a Todo:

It records your principle verbatim: vc-x1 assumes nothing about a repo's contents beyond `.jj` and `.vc-config.toml`. The design sketch: the repo declares its checks in `.vc-config.toml` (`[preflight] checks = [...]`), preflight runs what's declared and nothing when absent — the jj checks (tracking, bot-published, sync) always run since those *are* vc-x1's domain. 237	   `--check`; revisit push auto-rollback.** Push's preflight
238	   shells out to `vc-x1 sync --check` — a verify-only pass
239	   that is both racy (remote can move before the user's
240	   later apply) and not actually read-only (jj's fetch
241	   auto-ffs tracked bookmarks). Follow-up to the 0.67.0
242	   single-mode sync cycle.
243	   - Preflight becomes a real in-process sync (stop-on-error
244	     halts the push before anything is committed); the
245	     `sync --check` shell-out and PATH dependency go away.
246	   - Remove the deprecated hidden `--check` alias once
247	     nothing invokes it.
248	   - Apply the stop-on-error + `vc-x1 revert` philosophy to
249	     push's commit-stage rollback (today it auto-runs
250	     `jj op restore`, hiding the evidence).
251	5. **push preflight: repo-declared checks, not hardcoded
252	   cargo.** vc-x1 should assume nothing about a repo's
253	   contents beyond `.jj` and `.vc-config.toml` (principle
254	   stated 2026-07-15), but push's preflight hardcodes
255	   `cargo fmt` / `cargo clippy -D warnings` / `cargo test`
256	   — assuming every workspace is a Rust project. The
257	   fixtures dodge their own tool's preflight (`--from
258	   review`, "no Cargo.toml in the fixture"); any non-Rust
259	   project hits the same wall.
260	   - The repo declares its checks in `.vc-config.toml`
261	     (e.g. `[preflight] checks = [...]`); preflight runs
262	     what's declared, nothing when absent — the jj checks
263	     (tracking, bot-published, sync) always run.
264	   - This project declares its cargo cycle there, keeping
265	     the per-commit protocol enforced while the tool goes
266	     content-agnostic.
267	   - Fixtures can then run real preflights (drop the
268	     `--from review` dodge where it was only about cargo).
269	6. **validate-numbering: rename the pair, check all
270	   sequence-managed notes files generically.** `validate-todo`
271	   / `fix-todo` only operate on the single file passed, so a
272	   renumber slip in `bugs.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, or
273	   `todo.md`'s `## Ideas` section passes unnoticed — too weak
274	   for a pre-commit gate. Prereq for the pre-commit doc
275	   validators (Todo "pre-commit: single rule ...").
276	   - Rename the pair: `validate-todo` → `validate-numbering`,

`--check`; revisit push auto-rollback.** Push's preflight
   shells out to `vc-x1 sync --check` — a verify-only pass
   that is both racy (remote can move before the user's
   later apply) and not actually read-only (jj's fetch
   auto-ffs tracked bookmarks). Follow-up to the 0.67.0
   single-mode sync cycle.
   - Preflight becomes a real in-process sync (stop-on-error
     halts the push before anything is committed); the
     `sync --check` shell-out and PATH dependency go away.
   - Remove the deprecated hidden `--check` alias once
     nothing invokes it.
   - Apply the stop-on-error + `vc-x1 revert` philosophy to
     push's commit-stage rollback (today it auto-runs
     `jj op restore`, hiding the evidence).
5. **push preflight: repo-declared checks, not hardcoded
   cargo.** vc-x1 should assume nothing about a repo's
   contents beyond `.jj` and `.vc-config.toml` (principle
   stated 2026-07-15), but push's preflight hardcodes
   `cargo fmt` / `cargo clippy -D warnings` / `cargo test`
   — assuming every workspace is a Rust project. The
   fixtures dodge their own tool's preflight (`--from
   review`, "no Cargo.toml in the fixture"); any non-Rust
   project hits the same wall.
   - The repo declares its checks in `.vc-config.toml`
     (e.g. `[preflight] checks = [...]`); preflight runs
     what's declared, nothing when absent — the jj checks
     (tracking, bot-published, sync) always run.
   - This project declares its cargo cycle there, keeping
     the per-commit protocol enforced while the tool goes
     content-agnostic.
   - Fixtures can then run real preflights (drop the
     `--from review` dodge where it was only about cargo).
6. **validate-numbering: rename the pair, check all
   sequence-managed notes files generically.** `validate-todo`
   / `fix-todo` only operate on the single file passed, so a
   renumber slip in `bugs.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, or
   `todo.md`'s `## Ideas` section passes unnoticed — too weak
   for a pre-commit gate. Prereq for the pre-commit doc
   validators (Todo "pre-commit: single rule ...").
   - Rename the pair: `validate-todo` → `validate-numbering`,

1	# Todo
2	
3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.
5	
6	Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
7	line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
8	pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.
9	
10	## In Progress
11	
12	When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
13	problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
14	by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
15	   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
16	   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
17	   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
18	   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation
19	
20	**feat: inline session push + squash-push (0.69.0)**
21	
22	Push's `finalize-claude` stage detaches a delayed child to
23	squash+push `.claude`; a sandboxed bot run kills the child at
24	command exit — silently, every cycle (see Bugs #1 [[24]]). The
25	empty-@ goal behind the detach/delay dance is self-referential
26	for the bot (finalizing is itself session data), so the bot
27	forgoes it; only the user, acting after the turn, can capture
28	the full tail.
29	
30	Design notes:
31	- naming (decided 2026-07-15): `finalize` → `squash-push`
32	  - mechanism-named and repo-generic: a squash `@ → @-` +
33	    push
34	  - needed frequently on the bot repo (the session tail)
35	  - occasionally useful on the app repo: amend-and-push —
36	    a deliberate published-history rewrite + forced update
37	- squash-push of a just-pushed session commit rewrites it,
38	  so its push is a forced update
39	  - functions fine whether the bot or the user runs it
40	  - but on the bot repo only the user gets an empty `@`:
41	    the bot's own invocation writes new session data, so
42	    its `@` is non-empty again immediately
43	  - on any repo without a live writer it works as expected
44	    for anyone
45	- CLI shape
46	  - `-R`/`--repo`, default `.` (house convention: `-R`
47	    repo, `-r` revision)
48	  - positional BOOKMARK defaulting to `main` (mirrors push)
49	  - repo stays a flag — no command takes a positional repo
50	- no-op feedback
51	  - `@` empty and bookmark already at remote → "repo is
52	    already sync'd with remote", exit 0
53	  - `@` empty but remote behind → skip the squash, still
54	    push
55	- the bot may also run `squash-push` as a separate later
56	  command to capture the `vc-x1 push` record (leaves only
57	  its own)
58	
59	Plan:
60	- 0.69.0-0 prep: backfill Commits:, bump version, pick up
61	  Todo #1, open chores section (done)
62	- 0.69.0-1 push: session squash+push inline (done)
63	  - replace the finalize-claude shell-out with an
64	    in-process squash of the trailing session writes +
65	    `jj git push --bookmark main -R .claude`
66	  - a session-push failure is a visible push failure
67	  - crate temporarily named `vc-x1-dev` so per-commit
68	    installs don't clobber the stable `vc-x1` another bot
69	    instance uses
70	  - tests
71	- 0.69.0-2 squash-push: rename `finalize` → `squash-push` (done)
72	  - zero-ceremony default: `-R .`, BOOKMARK positional
73	    defaulting to `main`, no-op feedback
74	  - retire `--detach` / `--delay`
75	  - module + types follow: `finalize.rs` →
76	    `squash_push.rs`, `FinalizeArgs` / `FinalizeParams` →
77	    `SquashPushArgs` / `SquashPushParams`
78	  - `finalize_inline` dissolves: with the detach branch
79	    gone, preflight + exec is the command's only path, so
80	    the shim merges into the renamed entry point
81	  - failure-marker machinery (decided at -2): retired
82	    entirely with `--detach` — markers only existed for the
83	    detached child's invisible exit; any stale ones under
84	    `~/.cache/vc-x1/finalize-status` are inert (delete by
85	    hand)
86	  - rename push stage `finalize-claude` →
87	    `squash-push-bot` (first use of the work/bot stage
88	    vocabulary; the remaining app/claude stages sweep at
89	    -4); `--no-finalize` → `--no-squash-push`
90	  - deprecated `finalize` alias (decided at -2): none —
91	    the flag surface changed incompatibly, so an alias
92	    would trade a clear "unrecognized subcommand" for
93	    confusing flag errors
94	  - `Context.log` follows `--detach` out: its only reader
95	    was the detach re-exec, so the field and `load(log)`
96	    parameter are gone
97	  - tests
98	  - docs pulled forward from -4: cycle-protocol.md
99	    stop-and-wait + Recovery rewritten for the inline
100	    world; AGENTS.md scrubbed of "finalize" as a plain
101	    word (collides with the retired command name)
102	- 0.69.0-3 bot-repo published backstop (done)
103	  - invariant (clarified 2026-07-15): at rest the bot
104	    repo's `main == main@origin`; the bookmark only moves
105	    inside a push / squash-push run, which publishes it in
106	    the same invocation — an at-rest mismatch means a
107	    previous publish was lost (or never happened)
108	  - new `vc-x1 validate-bot` (name chosen 2026-07-15):
109	    read-only check of that invariant + tracking; distinct
110	    message when `main@origin` doesn't exist (never
111	    pushed); non-zero exit on any finding; no cargo steps
112	    — cheap enough for routine use (reacquaint, timers)
113	  - push preflight runs the check and errors (decided
114	    2026-07-15: no automatic fixing) — placed before the
115	    sync / cargo steps so it fails fast; resolve with
116	    `vc-x1 squash-push -R .claude`, rerun push
117	  - squash-push detects the same condition and reports it
118	    ("an earlier publish was likely lost"), then proceeds —
119	    publishing is its job, so healing is not auto-fixing
120	  - shared machinery in common.rs: `PublishState` +
121	    `bookmark_publish_state` + `verify_bot_published`
122	  - tests; new `test_helpers::jj_ok` for new tests instead
123	    of a 4th hand-rolled jj helper copy (migrating the old

125	  - the "run at every vc-x1 invocation, config-gated" idea
126	    is queued as its own Todo (a could, not a should)
127	  - push preflight's hardcoded cargo fmt/clippy/test removed
128	    (decided 2026-07-15): vc-x1 assumes nothing about a
129	    repo's contents beyond `.jj` and `.vc-config.toml`; a
130	    project that wants pre-push checks runs them explicitly
131	    before `vc-x1 push` (AGENTS.md's cargo cycle already
132	    does) — preflight is now version-control checks only
133	    (tracking, bot-published, sync --check)
134	  - crate renamed back to `vc-x1` (decided 2026-07-15): the

138	- 0.69.0-4 docs: README (cycle-protocol's stop-and-wait +
139	  Recovery sections were rewritten at -2); repo-terminology
140	  sweep — "bot repo" / "work repo" (decided 2026-07-15)

142	  AGENTS.md, cycle-protocol.md, and code doc comments; push
143	  stage-name sweep to match (`commit-app` → `commit-work`,
144	  `commit-claude` → `commit-bot`, `push-app` → `push-work`;
145	  `squash-push-bot` landed at -2)
146	- 0.69.0 close-out and validation
147	
148	Continuity (resume 2026-07-15):

Both changes are in, all green. Summary:

**Cargo preflight removed from push** — `stage_preflight` is now version-control checks only: bookmark tracking (both repos), the bot-published backstop, and the `sync --check` divergence check. The `cargo fmt` / `clippy` / `test` steps are gone, and the doc comment records the principle: vc-x1 assumes nothing about a repo's contents beyond `.jj` and `.vc-config.toml`; a project that wants pre-push checks runs them explicitly before invoking `vc-x1 push` (which is exactly what AGENTS.md's per-commit cargo cycle already does — nothing changes about this project's protocol, only who enforces it). Updated the stale mentions: `Stage::Preflight` doc, push's `--help` stage list (now says "no build steps; run project checks yourself before pushing"), dry-run message, and the fixture comments that dodged the cargo wall (`--from message` is still needed in unit tests, but for a different reason — preflight's `sync --check` re-invokes `current_exe()`, which under `cargo test` is the test harness). The Todo #5 I'd queued ("repo-declared checks in config") is deleted — your decision supersedes it, and the list renumbered back.

**Crate renamed back to `vc-x1`** — `Cargo.toml` name restored, temp comment removed. Cargo cycle green: clippy clean, all 426 tests pass, `cargo install` replaced the stable `vc-x1` 0.69.0-0 on PATH with 0.69.0-3, and I removed the obsolete `vc-x1-dev` binary (`cargo uninstall vc-x1-dev`) since the dual-instance window is closed. Smoke-tested the installed binary: `vc-x1 validate-bot` reports published, and `vc-x1 push --dry-run` shows the slimmed preflight.

The -3 ladder entry records both decisions with the why. Ready for the commit description when you are.

content.text:
**Title** (44 chars):

```
feat: bot-repo published backstop (0.69.0-3)
```

**Body:**

```
At rest the bot repo's `main` always matches `main@origin` — the
bookmark only moves inside a push / squash-push run, which
publishes it in the same invocation. An at-rest mismatch therefore
means an earlier publish was lost (Bugs #1 sat invisible for 8
cycles). This commit makes the invariant checkable and checked:
new `vc-x1 validate-bot` (read-only, two jj lookups), push
preflight errors on a mismatch (no automatic fixing — the user
resolves with `vc-x1 squash-push -R .claude`), and squash-push
reports the condition then proceeds, since publishing is its job.

Two decisions ride along: push preflight's hardcoded cargo
fmt/clippy/test is removed — vc-x1 assumes nothing about a repo's
contents beyond `.jj` and `.vc-config.toml`; projects run their
own checks before pushing — and the crate is renamed back to
`vc-x1` (the vc-x1-dev dual-install window closed).

- src/validate_bot.rs: new subcommand — tracking +
  published checks on the bot repo (-R, default .claude);
  non-zero exit on any finding; parse + fixture tests
- src/common.rs: PublishState, bookmark_publish_state()
  (present() maps a missing remote bookmark to NeverPushed),
  verify_bot_published() error wrapper
- src/push.rs: preflight = verify-tracking + bot-published +
  sync --check only; cargo steps removed; Stage::Preflight
  and stage docs updated
- src/main.rs: ValidateBot wiring + long_about; push help
  stage list notes preflight has no build steps
- src/squash_push.rs: report an at-rest mismatch (warn) or
  never-pushed bookmark (info) before proceeding; heal test
- src/test_helpers.rs: jj_ok() promoted for new tests (old
  per-module copies stay with the jj-facade Todo)
- src/push/integration_tests.rs: preflight-errors test; the
  --from message rationale reworded (current_exe under
  cargo test, no longer "no Cargo.toml")
- tests/cli_sync.rs: --from review comment updated
- notes/todo.md: -3 done with decisions recorded; new Todo
  "Run validate-bot at every vc-x1 invocation" (a could,
  not a should); continuity refreshed
- notes/chores/chores-13.md: 0.69.0 Commits: backfilled
  (-0, -1, -2)
- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: crate renamed vc-x1-dev -> vc-x1;
  version 0.69.0-3
```

Awaiting your review before committing/pushing.

content.text:
Pushed — all stages completed; 0.69.0-3 is on `main` in both repos, state cleared. Two observations from the run:

1. The first attempt died on a transient `.git/index.lock` race at `bookmark-set`; push's rollback restored both repos cleanly, and `--restart` reran everything successfully. Good live exercise of the rollback path.

2. The new squash-push warning fired *inside* push's `squash-push-bot` stage — "'main' does not match 'main@origin' — an earlier publish was likely lost". That's a false alarm in this context: two stages earlier, `bookmark-set` legitimately moved `.claude main`, and it simply hadn't been pushed yet — the expected mid-command window, observed from inside the command. The report was designed for standalone at-rest invocations; when squash-push runs as push's final stage the mismatch is the normal state. Worth a small follow-up (suppress or soften the report when constructed by push's stage). I'll note it at the next cycle-start unless you want it handled differently.

Stopping here so you can fold the session tail (`vc-x1 squash-push -R .claude`).
